items.name: Absorbing Shield
items.description: A ring of runes runs along this shield's circumference. While holding this shield, you gain a +2 bonus to AC. This shield has 6 charges, and it regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you take damage that isn't bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing damage, you can use your reaction to expend one charge to produce the effects of a 1st-level Absorb Elements spell, but it can also absorb force, necrotic, poison, or radiant damage. This feature does not function if the triggering damage is paired with bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing damage.

items.name: Accretion Beam
items.description:
An unstable device that outputs a staggering amount of energy the longer it remains active.

The weapon has a normal range of 60 feet and a long range of 180 feet, and it has the two-handed property. It deals 1 fire damage on a hit.

items.name: Aci Feather
items.description: A beautiful blue and white feather that when worn ones hair will billow majestically, even if there is no wind present as a bonus action; works with the cloak of billowing.

items.name: Alchemist's Soothing Tea
items.description: When measuring and balancing the herbal specimens \[Devil's Bloodleaf leaves and Phorus Blossom nectar\] needed to make this tea, 2d4 + 2 bags of magical tea can be created with each 'batch'. You can steep a bag of tea for 1 minute in a cup of boiling water to transform the water into a rich, flavorful tea that calms the nerves and revitalizes the spirit. When you drink a cup of the hot tea, you gain 2d6 + 2 temporary hit points. In addition, you may roll with advantage on saving throws caused by any effect causing you to be charmed or frightened for 10 minutes. You can drink the tea using an action, although it's most commonly finished over the course of at least 1 minute. If the tea has been left to cool \[10 minutes+\] before you drink it, you regain only half the amount of temporary hit points, and no other benefits are present.

items.name: Alchemy Blade
items.description:
This sword has a glass channel running down its length. In place of a traditional pommel, it instead has a open hole designed to fit atop a typical flask or vial. While holding the sword, you can use an action to attach a flask or vial to the sword and draw its contents into its blade; doing so changes the sword's properties based on the attached flask or vial.

*

items.name: Amentum
items.description: While wearing the Amentum, you can throw a weapon with the Thrown property a further distance equal to the object's initial minimum distance. Additionally, the Amentum corrects an object's trajectory while in flight. If the player rolls a 1 on a ranged attack roll from a weapon with the thrown property, while wearing the Amentum, they may choose to reroll, but must take the results of the second roll. This feature can be used three times, resetting at dawn.

items.name: Arcane Core
items.description:
This wondrous, arcane-fueled stone bearing the power rune, can be fitted to any tiny object, this process takes an hour to complete. Once the item is complete the item becomes a weapon that can only be thrown. The weapon has the following properties:

**Thrown:** (20/40),

**Damage:** 1d6\[magical bludgeoning\]

The item appears normal but can be detected using spells like detect magic. It uses your Dexterity or Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls.

**Invoking the Rune.**  When you make an attack with the item and hit, it deals an additional 2d6 force damage to the target. Any creature or object within 5 feet of the impact must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they take 3d6 force damage and are pushed back 10 feet from the point of impact. On a successful save, they take half damage and are not pushed back.

Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.

[](/img/wiki/hexcore.png)



If the attack misses, it will travel to max thrown range and can be picked up and used.

items.name: Arcane Filter
items.description: This quante, sphere shaped metal device is just big enough to be held in one hand when inactivate. When activated, either by a command word or by a button activation on the side of the object, it unfurls and begins to hover soundlessly 5 feet off the ground near where it was activated. It can be pushed or moved out of the way, and will magically slow down to avoid collision if pushed into a wall or person. It has a 12 in AC, and 12 hit points with resistance to all damage types, but immune to psychic damage. While active, it spends 10 minutes turning all available air in it's facility into comfortable, clean and breathable air and will constantly renew this process and keep the area as comfortable as possible until destroyed or deactivated. Once it has been active for 24 hours, it cannot be used again until the next dawn. This item cannot be used in the middle of combat to filter poisonous air, such as by a Green Dragon's Breath weapon, but can clean extra dimensional spaces such as a bag of holding.

items.name: Arcane Guardian
items.description:
## Description


*Often called Arcane Guardians these sorcerers take a backseat to the events of life, letting others shine while they lift them higher, watching and protecting them. Many a hero was made by the hands of an Arcane Guardian.*

Features:

*   [1st Level - Guardian Magic](#1st-level---guardian-magic)
*   [1st Level - Loving Protection](#1st-level---loving-protection)
*   [6th Level - Gifting Inner Strength](#6th-level---gifting-inner-strength)
*   [14th Level - Altruistic Splinter](#14th-level---altruistic-splinter)
*   [18th Level - Arcane Enlightenment](#18th-level---arcane-enlightenment)

## 1st Level - Guardian Magic

You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown on the Guardian table. Each of these spells counts as a sorcerer spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of sorcerer spells you know.

Whenever you gain a sorcerer level, you can replace one spell you gained from this feature with another spell of the same level. The new spell must be an abjuration or a transmutation spell from the sorcerer, warlock, or wizard spell list.

### Arcane Guardian Spells

*   1st: bless, protection from evil and good
*   3rd: aid, pass without trace
*   5th: aura of vitality, protection from energy
*   7th: aura of life, aura of purity
*   9th: mass cure wounds, skill empowerment

## 1st Level - Loving Protection

Starting at 1st level, your selfless nature begins to show in your spellcasting. Whenever an ally within 30 feet of you fits the casting requirements for a spell with a casting time of 1 Reaction, you can spend an amount of sorcery points equal to the spell level to cast the spell and give the effects to them. You can do this an amount of times equal to your Charisma modifier, resetting when you finish a long rest.

## 6th Level - Gifting Inner Strength

Starting at 6th level, you harness the ability to spread your talents to your allies. Once per turn, as a bonus action, you can strengthen an ally's next spell or attack with a Metamagic option you have access to, spending sorcery points as you would to use the options normally. These options last for a minute. The viable Metamagic options are as listed:

### Quickened Assault

When your target casts a spell that has a casting time of 1 action, they can choose to change the casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting.

Alternatively, they can make one melee or ranged weapon attack as a bonus action.

### Subtle Assault

When the target casts a spell, they can choose to cast it without any somatic or verbal components.

Alternatively, they can give themself advantage on one melee or ranged weapon attack roll.

### Empowered Assault

When the target rolls damage for a spell or weapon attack, they can reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). They must use the new rolls.

### Heightened Assault

When the target casts a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, they can give one target of the spell disadvantage on its first saving throw made against the spell.

Alternatively, if they make a melee or ranged weapon attack and hit, they can add your Charisma modifier to one of the damage rolls (minimum of one).

## 14th Level - Altruistic Splinter

Starting at 14th level, you learn to manipulate the bounds of your sorcery. Whenever you cast a spell that has a range of Self, you can spend an amount of sorcery points equal to twice the spell's level to change the range to Touch as long as the target is a willing creature. The spell level limit of this feature is half your Charisma modifier.  You can do this an amount of times equal to half your proficiency bonus, resetting when you finish a long rest.

## 18th Level - Arcane Enlightenment

Starting at 18th level, you gain the ability to funnel your strength into your allies. As an action on your turn, you can boost your allies' power until the end of your next turn. Every creature of your choice that is currently under the effects of a concentration spell cast by you receives the foll…

items.name: Arcane Tent
items.description:
This wondrous item takes the form of a normal-sized two-person tent. Setting up the tent takes ten minutes of effort. What appears to be an ordinary tent, yurt, or otherwise culturally appropriate transient dwelling from the outside is revealed to be significantly larger on the inside. Upon stepping through the entrance flap the individual finds themselves in a larger than expected space - containing 240 sqft of floor room and a 15ft high ceiling, with a small kitchen, separated outhouse, and a central hearth and sitting area. There are enough beds and accommodations for 4-6 people to sleep comfortably, even if more may fit physically. Rain will not enter through the roof, but the tent is not waterproof and will flood if submerged. Air also enters the tent from outside, leaving those within vulnerable to suffocation or otherwise if something should happen to that air.

If the tent is punctured or damaged to a significant degree (AC 11, HP 15, resistant to all damage except fire and acid), the magic will cease to function until repaired with the mending spell. All creatures and objects inside will be dumped into the space of the two-person tent, possibly breaking it further, and until repaired it will function like the tent it appears to be. Additionally, the tent has a number of furnishings and furniture which are bound to it; these will remain inside at all times, and cannot be removed. If destroyed, the furniture will reappear the next time the tent is set up. Any objects left inside will be dumped out after the tent is dismantled.

items.name: Arcanist's Magnificent Magnifier
items.description: This magical magnifying glass helps deduce the arcane nature of items. While looking through the glass at an object within 1 foot of you, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any Intelligence (Arcana) or Intelligence (Investigation) check you make regarding that object’s magical properties if your proficiency bonus is not doubled already. In addition, you can speak the glass’s command word as an action to cast the "identify" spell from it, targeting an object with the spell that you can see through the glass within 1 foot of you. Each time you do, there is a cumulative 5 percent chance that the glass shatters and is destroyed after casting the spell.

items.name: Archivist's Tome
items.description: The pages in this tome are crafted so they never run out. When the user fills up the pages within the tome, the book automatically creates new, blank pages. The older pages are automatically erased from the pages and stored, and the contents will reappear on command.

items.name: Armament Tattoo
items.description: Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo depicts an intricately designed weapon.

items.name: Arrow's Reach Talisman
items.description: While attuned to this talisman, your ranged weapon attacks no longer suffer disadvantage at long range. This is not limited to bows, but also affects crossbows, spears, javelins, daggers, etc.

items.name: Artificer's Blueprint
items.description:
This item appears as a rolled-up scroll of blue parchment paper with white gridlines at regular intervals. When unfurled, a creature holding it may use an action and envision a creature, landscape, or anything they can think of the blueprint projects a three-dimensional illusion of the thing envisioned as a rough sketch of lines, tracing the contours and edges of the item. The illusion can be freely tampered with through intuitive hand gestures, such as enlarging or reducing, rotating and turning. Changing the illusion itself requires a bonus action. The illusion lasts for 10 minutes starting from the time it was left inactive or deactivated with a bonus action. A creature has advantage on the check (if needed) when creating or constructing something according to the blueprint (DM's discretion).

The extent of the detail the illusion has is limited, it appears as white lines, and grants the observer no knowledge they otherwise wouldn't have, such as the inner workings of a specific lock they have not disassembled.

items.name: Audioshard
items.description:
The Audioshard is the audio version of the Spellshard. As an action, the user can activate the shard which allows it to store sound up to a max of 10 ft away, determined whenever the user activates the shard. The shard can store up to 40 hours of audio.

The user can also take an action to play any recorded audio which can be played at a volume ranging from a whisper to a scream.

\*Artisans Guild invention

items.name: Augmenter
items.description:
## Description

The Augmenter is a unique blend of artificer and psychic, merging technology with mental prowess. These specialists understand the psyche and wield their own bodies as tools for both combat and manipulation. Whether augmenting their limbs or delving into the minds of others, Augmenters are masters of self-enhancement


*   [3rd Level - Augmenter Spells](#3rd-level---augmenter-spells)
*   [3rd Level - Self Augmentation](#3rd-level---self-augmentation)
*   [5th Level - Extra Attack](#5th-level---extra-attack)
*   [9th Level - Mind Over Matter](#9th-level---mind-over-matter)
*   [15th Level - Master Augmenter](#15th-level---master-augmenter)
*   [Augmenter Infusions](#augmenter-infusions)

## 3rd Level - Augmenter Spells

You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Augmenter Spells table. These spells count as artificer spells for you, but they don’t count against the number of artificer spells you prepare.

Augmenter Spells reflect your understanding of the psyche and allows you to influence the mind of others.

**Augmenter Spells**

3rd Level - Command, Dissonant Whispers

5th Level - Tasha’s Mind Whip, Crown of Madness

9th Level - Antagonize, Fast Friends

13th Level - Raulothim’s Psychic Lance, Compulsion

17th Level - Synaptic Static, Modify Memory

## 3rd Level - Self Augmentation

Starting at 3rd level, you master the technique of augmenting your own body. Each of the following parts of your body can be infused using your artificer Infusions; Torso, Head, Legs. You also gain access to Augmenter Infusions, these infusions are known and does not count toward your total of known infusions. For the purposes of normal Infusions, the Torso is seen as Armor, head as a Helmet or any peripheral that is worn on the head, and legs are seen as boots.

You also learn how to augment your arms with special arm weapons. These can also be infused, and you can only have one arm enhancement active at a time. Whenever you finish a long rest you can swap arm enhancements.

### **Arm Enhancements**

These Enhancements count as weapons and infusions can be applied to them. Each enhancement grants the artificer an applicable fighting Style based on the installed enhancement.

#### **Mantis Blade**

You gain retractable blade attachments on either of your arms

**Properties**: Finesse

**Fighting Style:** Dueling

**Damage**: 1d6 piercing

**Special**: The blade can be retracted or unsheathed as a bonus action, leaving the hand free to wield other items. When you score a critical hit with the mantis blade the target needs to make a constitution save against your Spell DC or start to bleed. When bleeding the target loses hp equal to your proficiency bonus at the start of each of its turn. It can make the save again at the end of each turn.

#### **Monowire Attachment**

These fine wires get pulled from your wrists and are wielded like a whip.

**Properties**: Finesse, Reach

**Fighting Style**: Two-Weapon Fighting

**Damage**: 1d4 slashing

**Special**: The wires count as two separate weapons for the purpose of two weapon fighting, but only require a single infusion. The wires use Intelligence for its attack and damage modifiers.

#### **Gorilla Arms**

Your Hands and forearms are augmented with heavier metals and mechanical support.

**Properties**: Heavy

**Fighting Style**: Unarmed Fighting

**Damage**: Unarmed Strike, Bludgeoning

**Special**: You make your attacks with the Gorilla Arms as unarmed strikes. You also have advantage on Strength( Athletics) checks to escape, attempt, maintain grapples.

#### **Arm Launcher**

You can load bolts or sling bullets into your arm and fire them as projectiles.

**Properties**: Ranged (40/80), Reload (6)

**Fighting Style**: Archery

**Damage**: 1d8 piercing (bolts), bludgeoning (sling bullets)

**Special**: The Launcher leaves your hands open while firing. On a hit, the launcher does 1d4 force damage to the target, all creatures within 5ft of the target must make Dexterity save against your spell save DC or take 1d4…

items.name: Avenger
items.description:
Avengers seek to right a wrong, stopping at nothing to correct a past sleight. They dole out their revenge with their own hands, using steel just as much as spirit to forward their goals.

## Soul of Vengeance

At 3rd level, you gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons, and you can use weapons as your spiritual focus. When you activate your spirit tether, you gain an amount of temporary hit points equal to 1d6 + half your spiritualist level (rounded down).

## Avenger Arts

Also at 3rd level, you gain access to new spirit arts that reflect your soul’s hunger for vengeance.

## Duel of the Fates

As an action, you can spend 1 spirit point to initiate a spirit duel with your target. The target must make a Charisma saving throw or be blinded. While the target is blinded this way, you are also blinded, except you and your target can still see each other normally. As long as this blindness persists, your weapon attacks deal an additional 1d6 damage.

### Spirit Shield

When you are attacked by your tethered target while you are holding a shield, you can use your reaction to spend 1 spirit point and force the target to make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the attack misses, and the next attack you make against the tethered target has advantage.

### Phantom Pain

As an action, you can spend 2 spirit points to share your suffering with the tethered target. For the next minute, whenever you take damage, the target takes half as much necrotic damage.

## Extra Attack

Beginning at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. Moreover, you can use a spirit art that uses an action in place of one of these attacks.

## Avenging Link

At 13th level, your soul lashes out at any creature that would harm you or your allies. Whenever a creature you can see within range of your spirit tether damages you or an ally within 30 feet, you can use your reaction to immediately create a spirit tether. The creature must be one of the targets.

## Master Avenger

At 18th level, you master the art of vengeance. You learn the following spirit art:

### Soul Coating

You can use 5 spirit points as an action to empower your weapon. For the next minute, whenever you hit a tethered target with a weapon attack, the attack deals an additional 3d6 force damage. The first time you hit a tethered target after using this art, it must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the target is rended by your spirit, and cannot restore hit points or gain temporary hit points as long as your spirit tether lasts.

items.name: Bag of Colding
items.description: This item resembles a bag of holding, but is cool to the touch and emits visibly cold air while opened. It can only hold 100 pounds of items, but any perishable items do not degrade while within the bag. It is otherwise identical to a bag of holding.

items.name: Balance
items.description: **Magic Weapon.

items.name: Bandolier of Scroll Storing
items.description: This bandolier comes equipped with a single scroll case, inside of which up to 100 levels worth of scrolls can be stored. The wearer of this bandolier can produce a scroll from this case as part of the action used to cast the spell.

items.name: Bard-in-a-Box
items.description: This small music box can play songs at the request of the holder. As a bonus action, you can command the box to play a song that it knows, causing the contraption to emit a slightly tinny rendition of the musical number at a moderate volume. The box can know up to five songs at a time, and can not recreate lyrics or other creature sounds. It has a small knob on the side that adjusts the box's volume, being as quiet as a whisper to as loud as a scream. Sheet music can be fed into the box through a narrow slot along the bottom of the device. Sheet music must be labeled with a song title, page number, and instrument in order to be replayed. Pages failing to meet the requirements causes the box to make a sad, trumpeting noise, and are spat back out. Once a sheet is successfully fed into the box, it is consumed and cannot be retrieved. If the new piece of music will cause the box to forget an older one, it quickly plays several bars of the now-forgotten number before slowing to a stop.

items.name: Barkers Bugle
items.description:
This bugle is magical, lined on it's edges with a faint swirling pattern which flares at the horn end. The unique grooves of the instrument allow it to reverberate sound more effectively. Condensing it and emerging as volumes louder than originally played from one end.

This item comes with an inter-changeable mouthpiece, which is much wider and allows speech through the piping.

Sounds made through this instrument are audible out to a distance of 300 feet. As an action once per day, you may choose to blare this instrument. Doing so requires creatures in a 30-foot cone originating from you to make a DC 13 constitution saving throw unless they are covering their ears. On a failure, they take 1d10 thunder damage are deafened for one minute. Objects in this area take 3d10 thunder damage. They may repeat this saving throw again at the end of their turns, ending the effect on a success.

items.name: Belt of Returning
items.description:
A finely crafted dark leather belt with two loops, each decorated with a shard of sapphire. The magic of the belt leaves an invisible mark on the surface of the weapons stowed in each loop and invisibly inscribes the name of the item on the sapphire shard.

Two one-handed weapons with the thrown property can be stowed in the belt. When you make ranged attack with a weapon marked by the belt, immediately after the attack, hit or miss, the weapon returns to the belt.

items.name: Black Cleaver
items.description:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

After you have made a successful weapon attack with this weapon, you gain an additional 10 feet movement speed which lasts until the end of that turn.

Additionally, you can also lower the defensive prowess of the one you are attacking. The battleaxe has 5 charges, and it regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you attack with this weapon, you can use your reaction to expend a charge to lower the target's AC by 2, includes against the triggering attack, until the start of its next turn. You can only use feature once per turn.

items.name: Bladed Cestus
items.description:
Proficiency with a cestus allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

Special: As a bonus action, you can make one attack with this weapon.

items.name: Blightstaff
items.description:
This magic staff was created from a long, straight branch from a petrified tree. It has a small animal skull on the top with thorns to mimic antlers, and two dark gems in the eye sockets.

While holding this staff, you gain a +2 bonus to the saving throw DCs of your Druid spells.

When you create an area of difficult terrain, any creature moving through it takes 1d4 necrotic damage for every 5 feet it moves in that area.

items.name: Blood Magic
items.description:
## Description


*The secrets of hemocraft and other forms of blood magic were once the sole purview of the mysterious Claret Orders. In recent years, however, these secrets have begun to spread beyond the order’s members and into the hands of less scrupulous mages. Blood magic has a bad reputation in Tal’Dorei, even though its powers can be used for much more than evil.*

**Features:**

*   [2nd Level - Blood Channeling](#2nd-level---blood-channeling)
*   [2nd Level - Sanguine Burst](#2nd-level---sanguine-burst)
*   [6th Level - Bond of Mutual Suffering](#6th-level---bond-of-mutual-suffering)
*   [10th Level - Glyph of Hemorrhaging](#10th-level---glyph-of-hemorrhaging)
*   [14th Level - Thicker than Water](#14th-level---thicker-than-water)

## 2nd Level - Blood Channeling

When you choose this arcane tradition at 2nd level, you are able to use your own depleted life essence to channel your magical abilities. Whenever your current hit points are below your hit point maximum, you can use your own body as an arcane focus.

In addition, when casting a wizard spell that requires a costly material component, you can forego the component by taking 1d10 necrotic damage per 50 gp of the cost of the component (minimum 1d10). This damage can’t be reduced in any way. If this damage reduces you to 0 hit points, the spell fails but the spell slot is not expended.

## 2nd Level - Sanguine Burst

Also at 2nd level, you learn how to weave your life force into a spell you cast, boosting its intensity at the cost of your vitality. Whenever you roll damage for a spell you’ve cast of 1st level or higher, you can choose to take necrotic damage equal to the spell’s level to reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Intelligence modifier (minimum one). This damage can’t be reduced in any way, and you must use the new rolls.

## 6th Level - Bond of Mutual Suffering

At 6th level, when a creature you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to bind your vitality to the attacker and force them to share your pain. The attacker takes damage equal to the damage you took.

This feature cannot be used against constructs or undead. You can use this feature once. You must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

At 14th level, you can use this feature twice between rests.

## 10th Level - Glyph of Hemorrhaging

Starting at 10th level, when you damage a creature with a spell, you can choose to curse that creature for 1 minute. While cursed in this way, whenever the creature is hit by an attack, it takes an extra 1d6 necrotic damage. At the end of each of the creature’s turns, it can make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, ending the curse on a success.

This feature cannot be used against creatures that are undead or constructs. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

## 14th Level - Thicker than Water

Upon reaching 14th level, the blood that flows through your veins is empowered with arcane vigor that mends wounds and helps preserve your life. Whenever a spell or magical effect causes you to regain hit points, you regain an additional number of hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.

In addition, while you are concentrating on a spell, you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

items.name: Bloodhunters Brand
items.description:
This symbol resembles that of the clan, coven, or circle that brought you into the blood hunting tradition to which you belong. It may be placed anywhere on your body. Typically, these are applied via the use of a needle and removed upon losing attunement to this item.

While wearing this brand, your hemomancy DC is improved by an amount corresponding to this item's rarity.

In addition, you may use a bonus action to regain a use of your blood maledict feature. This ability recharges at dawn.

items.name: Bloodneedle
items.description:
Bloodneedle sits in a sheath covered in glimmering dark rubies that always seem to be crusted in blood. It's blade appears to be hollow glass.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit with an attack, the target takes an extra 1d10 necrotic damage (ignores resistance and immunity).When you throw it and it hits or misses, it teleports instantly to your hand.

Whenever you roll a 1 on an attack roll using this weapon, you takes 3d8 necrotic damage(ignoring resistance and immunity). This damage decreases your maximum hit points until you have rolled a 20 with this weapon(ending attunement with the weapon does not removed the decreased hit points). You can also remove this using a greater restoration spell.

While you are holding Bloodneedle, you can use an action to cast the harm spell (save DC 15) from the weapon, and this action can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Body Back-up
items.description:
: Skeleton

*   Your wisdom score increases by one.
*   You can designate one inanimate skeleton by touch as an action. After 30 days, an invisible spiritual connection is made between you and the skeleton. If you are destroyed while such a connection is active, you rise again from the skeleton you had designated. You can only have one skeleton designated in this way at a time.

items.name: Book of Instant Copying
items.description:
This book has a glass cover and is typically found with 2d10 + 30 blank pages. As an action, you can place a piece of paper or similar object (such as a leaf or patterned fabric) between the book's glass and the page below it and speak the book's command word. When you do, the book magically inscribes an image of the placed object in black ink as it appears pressed against the glass on the first blank page of the book.

Copied magic scrolls or spellbooks appear as nonsensical glyphs when copied in this way. When the last page of the book has been used, you can rebind it with 50 blank new pages using 50 gp of fine materials and 8 hours of work.

items.name: Book of Misspells
items.description: This magical tome appears to contain several spell scrolls. Each scroll can be used once per day, and regenerates daily at dawn. If the scrolls are ever removed from the tome, they disappear. The spells are inscribed wrong, and their effects vary wildly from the intended. The spells are cast as normal, except have the following effects rather than their original effects:

items.name: Boomburst Hammer
items.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. Attacks against constructs and objects are made with advantage. The hammer has 10 charges. When you hit with a melee attack with this weapon, you can expend up to 3 of its charges. For each charge you expend, the target takes an extra 1d6 thunder damage. Alternatively, you can spend 5 of its charges for the target to take 3d6 thunder damage and force them to make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be stunned for 1 minute, creatures immune to thunder damage are unaffected by the stun and the stun cannot be applied if there is no sound. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of its turn. Recharge 1d6+4 charges at dawn.

items.name: Bottle of Storing
items.description: This magical ivory bottle can store up to 40 gallons of liquid at once (As much as 1 barrel), after adding any amount of liquid to the bottle, no other liquid can enter the bottle (meaning can't mix liquids, only liquids of the same type, eg Potion of Superior healing to Potion of Superior healing, salt water can't mix with water etc). The liquid can be poured or drank like any other container Time passes for the liquid inside as usual

items.name: Bow of Silence
items.description: This magical bow has the power to silence its victims. You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. On a successful hit the victim must make a dc15 charisma saving throw or be mute for 1d4 rounds. If they fail by 5 or more they won't be able to say the name of their assailant for 1d4 days. If the victim passes a save, they are immune to this effect for the next 24 hours.

items.name: Bulwark
items.description:
## The Bulwark Class

Outside the king’s chambers, still as an armor display, the royal guard stands post as they await the coming rebelion force. Ready to meet it head on, no matter the result.

A scrappy militiaman clad in chainmail stands on the battlefield, now alone and bloodied, having outlasted all others through wit and defensive positioning.

Beneath a greathelm, the dwarf’s face pools with sweat as he crosses the great desert, sent on a mission to deliver an important message that cannot afford to fall into the wrong hands

Bulwarks spend large portions of their lives covered in heavy armor to the point that they are seldom ever seen without it. Their mastery over using it fully to their advantage naturally comes as second nature, allowing them to outperform nearly any other. What truly distinguishes a bulwark from other users of heavy armor is their distinct focus on making the absolute most of the defense it offers, knowing how best to move themselves to avoid or block incoming assaults. Bulwarks are often kept as personal guards to high ranking officials, as front-liners in the field of battle, or even as couriers who’s senders need certainty their message will arrive. Adventuring parties who are graced with the inclusion of one of these masters of defense can rest assured that at least one of them will be making it back alive.

### The Best Offense...

A Bulwark will tend to focus fully on a defensive standpoint in battle, as an overtly pressing offense might lead to gaps in your attention. In fact, some Bulwarks even refrain from having an actual offense entirely, relying on those around them to defeat their enemies while they spend their attention and energy on lasting as long as possible.

### Creating a Bulwark

When creating a Bulwark, consider why your character might have needed to train such a rigorous defense. What training have they undergone to truly set themselves ahead of their peers? Perhaps you were a member of a royal guard, someone trained to protect at any cost. Or rather just a soldier who has taken to protecting themselves a bit further than most. A Bulwark could be anyone of the great strength needed to use heavy armors, and utilizes it to the fullest, and you get to decide just how your character does that.

### Multiclassing

When multiclassing with the Bulwark class, use the following:

 Strength 13 and Constitution 13

**Proficiencies Gained:** Light armor, Medium armor, Shields, and Heavy armor

## Bulwark Table

|Level || Proficiency Bonus || Features

| 1st  || +2 || Defensive Stance, Weapon Mastery

| 2nd  || +2 || Fortitude

| 3rd  || +2 || Defensive Specialization

| 4th  || +2 || Ability Score Improvement

| 5th  || +3 || Extra Attack, Emissary

| 6th  || +3 || Conditioning

| 7th  || +3 ||Specialization Feature

| 8th  || +3 ||Ability Score Improvement

| 9th  || +4 ||Dueling Stance

| 10th || +4 ||Mental Fortitude

| 11th || +4 ||Shattering Stance

| 12th || +4 ||Ability Score Improvement

| 13th || +5 ||Specialization Feature

| 14th || +5 ||Reinforcing Fortitude

| 15th || +5 ||Steel Endurance

| 16th || +5 ||Ability Score Improvement

| 17th || +6 ||Mountain Stance

| 18th || +6 ||Specialization Feature

| 19th || +6 ||Ability Score Improvement

| 20th || +6 ||Adamantine Endurance

## Class Features

As a Bulwark, you gain the following class features.

### Base Class Features

#### Hit Points

**Hit Dice:** 1d10 per bulwark level

**Hit Points at 1st Level:** 10 + your constitution modifier

**Hit Points at Higher Levels:** 1d10 (or 6) + your constitution modifier per Bulwark level after first.

#### Proficiencies

**Armor**: Light, Medium, and Heavy Armor, Shields

**Weapons**: Simple Weapons, Warhammers, Heavy Crossbows

**Tools**: none

**Saving Throws**: Constitution and Charisma

**Skills**: Choose two from Animal Handling, Athletics, Insight, Intimidation, Medicine, Persuasion, and Religion

#### Equipment

You start with the following equipment, in addition to any equipment granted by your background:

*   (a) a warhammer or (b) a simple weapon of your choi…

items.name: Buster Bat
items.description:
This bat is created from unknown origin and exudes devastating capabilities. It is made from a type of wood called Ironwood, an extremely hard but light wood when compared to oak wood and while remaining natural wood in almost every way. It is as strong, heavy, and resistant to fire as steel. You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, in addition to the following abilities.

*

items.name: Cestus
items.description: Proficiency with a cestus allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it. Special: As a bonus action, you can make one attack with this weapon.

items.name: Chakram
items.description:
30gp, 3lbs. --- d4 slashing - finesse, light, thrown (10/30),

Description

Proficiency with a chakram allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it. On a missed throw, a chakram returns to the thrower's hand.

**Finesse**: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

**Light**: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

**Thrown**: If a weapon has the thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

items.name: Champions Weapon
items.description:
This weapon carries the last breath and fighting spirit of old champions, buried with them in their tombs as they carry their greatest arms into the afterlife.

You have a +3 to attack and damage rolls made when using this weapon. While wielding this sword, the threshold required for you to score a critical hit with weapon attacks is reduced by 1.

items.name: Channel of Artistry
items.description:
[](/img/wiki/artistry_channeler.png)



Artistry Channeler

## Description


**Features**

*   [1st Level - Armaments of Artistry](#1st-level---armaments-of-artistry)
*   [1st Level - Elemental Savant](#1st-level---elemental-savant)
*   [6th Level - Weaving Strike](#6th-level---weaving-strike)
*   [11th Level - Beautiful Virtuosity](#11th-level---beautiful-virtuosity)
*   [11th Level - Harmonized Strikes](#11th-level---harmonized-strikes)
*   [17th Level - Culmination of Artistry](#17th-level---culmination-of-artistry)

## 1st Level - Armaments of Artistry

When you pick this channel at 1st level, you gain proficiency in shortswords, scimitars, and whips. You also gain proficiency in one type of artisan’s tools of your choice.

## 1st Level - Elemental Savant

Your use and control of elemental energy is beautiful to many, and distracting to your opponents. Starting at 1st level, when you hit a creature with an attack, you have resistance to the first attack they make against you until the beginning of your next turn. Additionally, you may imbue two weapons instead of one. The imbued damage types must be the same for both weapons.

## 6th Level - Weaving Strike

At 6th level, you can sculpt your elemental energy with your weapon, enhancing both your own movements and your energy’s potency. When you deal damage with your Elemental Imbuement, the next attack roll you make during this turn has advantage, and your movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks until the beginning of your next turn.

## 11th Level - Beautiful Virtuosity

At 11th level, the beauty of your movement and the flow of your energy is entrancing and inspiring to allies. When you deal damage with your Elemental Imbuement, you may choose a creature within 30 feet that can see you. That creature may add your Elemental Imbuement die to the next attack roll, saving throw, or ability check they make until the beginning of your next turn.

## 11th Level - Harmonized Strikes

At 11th level, if you hit with a weapon attack from your attack action and hit with a spell attack roll made with your Overchanneled Imbuement in one turn, you may take a second bonus action that turn.

## 17th Level - Culmination of Artistry

At 17th level, you’ve perfected your casting as a form of art. You may use your Flash Charge feature an unlimited amount of times per day. When you hit with an attack, you may use your Flash Charge feature as a reaction. When you do, the attack deals additional damage equal to two rolls of your new Elemental Imbuement die.

items.name: Channel of Containment
items.description:
## Description

Features:

*   [1st Level - Armaments of Containment](#1st-level---armaments-of-containment)
*   [6th Level - Brawl Binding](#6th-level---brawl-binding)
*   [11th Level - Elemental Fortitude](#11th-level---elemental-fortitude)
*   [11th Level - Extra Attack](#11th-level---extra-attack)
*   [17th Level - Culmination of Containment](#17th-level---culmination-of-containment)

## 1st Level - Armaments of Containment

When you pick this channel at 1st level, you gain proficiency in improvised weapons. You also gain proficiency in heavy armor. Elemental Core You learn to confine your arcane energy within your body, empowering your unarmed attacks. You may choose to Imbue your body with elemental energy as opposed to a weapon. While imbued, you gain the following benefits:

*   Your unarmed strikes use your Elemental Imbuement dice for its damage type.
*   When you attack with an unarmed strike, you may use your Intelligence modifier, rather than your strength modifier, for attack and damage rolls.
*   You gain resistance to the damage type you chose for your Imbuement
*   You can use your body as a focus for your Channeler spells

## 6th Level - Brawl Binding

Beginning at 6th level, when you use the attack action to grapple a creature, the target takes damage equal to one roll of your Elemental Imbuement die if the grapple succeeds. Whenever you take the attack action against the creature on subsequent turns, if you are still grappling the creature, you may choose to deal this damage again. Additionally, if you succeed on grappling a creature, you can use your Overchanneled Imbuement as a bonus action. Creatures you grapple have disadvantage on saving throws against spells you cast.

## 11th Level - Elemental Fortitude

At 11th level, your contained energy provides a defensive ward that sits just under your skin, protecting you from harm. When you are subjected to an effect that has you roll a saving throw to take half damage, if the damage type is acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

## 11th Level - Extra Attack

Beginning at 11th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn.

## 17th Level - Culmination of Containment

Beginning at 17th level, your arcane energy binds perfectly to your flesh. You gain the following benefits while Imbued:

*   You gain immunity to the damage type you chose for your Imbuement
*   Your walking speed increases by 20 feet
*   You no longer need to hit with an Imbued weapon attack to cast Channeler spells as a bonus action.
*   When you cast a Channeler spell, add your Intelligence modifier to your unarmed strike damage and any Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution checks you make until the end of your next turn.

items.name: Channel of Devastation
items.description:
[](/img/wiki/devastation_channeler.png)



Devastation Channeler

## Description


**Features**

*   [1st Level - Armaments of Devastation](#1st-level---armaments-of-devastation)
*   [1st level - Accelerant](#1st-level---accelerant)
*   [6th Level - Devastating Presence](#6th-level---devastating-presence)
*   [11th Level - Devastating Strikes](#11th-level---devastating-strikes)
*   [11th Level - Extra Attack](#11th-level---extra-attack)
*   [17th Level - Culmination of Devastation](#17th-level---culmination-of-devastation)

## 1st Level - Armaments of Devastation

When you pick this channel at 1st level, you gain proficiency in glaives, greataxes, greatswords, halberds, and pikes. You also gain proficiency in heavy armor and shields.

## 1st level - Accelerant

At 1st level, you’ve learned how to create an accelerant with your devastating energy, overcharging your attacks. When you hit with a melee weapon attack, you can force the target to roll a strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, the target is knocked prone and suffers an additional effect depending on your imbuement. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier. When you take a short or long rest, you regain all expended uses.

##### Accelerant Effect

| Damage Type | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Acid | The target’s AC is reduced by 1 until the end of your next turn. |
| Fire | The target takes additional fire damage equal to one roll of your Elemental Imbuement die. |
| Cold | The target’s speed is reduced by half of their maximum until the end of your next turn. |
| Lightning | You may force one other creature of your choice within 10 feet of the target to take lightning damage equal to two rolls of your Elemental Imbuement die. |
| Thunder | The target is pushed back 15 feet. |

## 6th Level - Devastating Presence

At 6th level, your mere presence is recognized as a threat, inciting opponents to focus their aggression on you. Each creature of your choice that starts its turn within 30 feet of you and can see you must roll a Wisdom saving throw against to your spell save DC. On a failure, the creature has disadvantage on attacks that don’t target you until the end of that creature’s turn. If a creature succeeds on this saving throw, they are immune to this effect for the next 24 hours.

## 11th Level - Devastating Strikes

Starting at 11th level, whenever you deal damage with your Elemental Imbuement or a channeler spell that shares a damage type with your chosen Elemental Imbuement, you ignore resistance to that damage type, and you may treat immunity to that damage type as resistance.

## 11th Level - Extra Attack

Beginning at 11th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn.

## 17th Level - Culmination of Devastation

At 17th level, you’ve wrought elemental devastation everywhere you’d gone, and have found the perfect way to channel it. When you hit with a melee weapon attack, you can reroll that attack against any number of other targets within 30ft, hitting each target simultaneously. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier. When you finish a long rest, you regain all expended uses.

items.name: Channel of Madness
items.description:
[](/img/wiki/madness.png)



Madness Channeler

## Description


**Features**

*   [1st Level - Armaments of Madness](#1st-level---armaments-of-madness)
*   [1st Level - Medium For Madness](#1st-level---medium-for-madness)
*   [6th Level - Braincharge](#6th-level---braincharge)
*   [11th Level - Mental Overchannel](#11th-level---mental-overchannel)
*   [11th Level - Psychic Armaments](#11th-level---psychic-armaments)
*   [17th Level - Culmination of Madness](#17th-level---culmination-of-madness)

## 1st Level - Armaments of Madness

When you pick this channel at 1st level, you gain proficiency in hand crossbows, heavy crossbows, and longbows.

## 1st Level - Medium For Madness

At 1st level, you may select two 1st level enchantment spells. You learn these spells and may cast each of them once per day without expending a spell slot. At 6th level, you may choose two 2nd level enchantment spells, two 3rd level enchantment spells at 11th level, and two 4th level enchantment spells at 17th level.

Additionally, you may pick psychic damage as the damage type for your Elemental Imbuement.

## 6th Level - Braincharge

At 6th level, you’re able to overload the minds of others, driving them further towards insanity. When a creature fails the first save from an enchantment spell you cast, they take extra psychic damage equal to your Elemental Imbuement die. Then, you may have one creature you can see within 30 feet of you gain advantage on the next Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw they make until the beginning of your next turn.

## 11th Level - Mental Overchannel

At 11th level, when you deal psychic damage to a creature, you may choose to deal maximum psychic damage rather than rolling. If you do, the target is stunned until the beginning of your next turn. You cannot use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

## 11th Level - Psychic Armaments

At 11th level, when you imbue a weapon with psychic damage, you can change that weapon’s base damage type to psychic for as long as it’s imbued. Add your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1) to the damage of your weapon attacks imbued with psychic damage.

## 17th Level - Culmination of Madness

At 17th level, it’s hard to tell if you’re still sane or not; your internal energy has corroded your mind past the point of no return. You gain advantage on saving throws against enchantment spells. In addition, you gain immunity to psychic damage and immunity to effects that would charm or frighten you. Once per day, when you cast an enchantment spell, if it would target only one creature and no other creatures, you can instead make it target as many creatures as you can see within 60 feet of you.

items.name: Channel of Volatility
items.description:
## Description

The Channel of Volatility is a controversial path to undertake, as though it is the easiest channel to ‘master’, it may prove most perilous to the channeler using it. Those who practice the channel of Volatility must exercise extreme caution, else they risk losing control of their magic to horrific consequence.

:

*   [1st Level - Armaments of Volatility](#1st-level---armaments-of-volatility)
*   [1st Level - Volatile Charge](#1st-level---volatile-charge)
*   [6th Level - Volatile Surge](#6th-level---volatile-surge)
*   [11th Level - Risk and Reward](#11th-level---risk-and-reward)
*   [11th Level - Extra Attack](#11th-level---extra-attack)
*   [17th Level - Culmination of Volatility](#17th-level---culmination-of-volatility)

## 1st Level - Armaments of Volatility

When you pick this channel at 1st level, you gain proficiency in flails, mauls, and warhammers. You also gain proficiency in heavy armor and shields.

## 1st Level - Volatile Charge

Your internal energy is difficult to predict and control, yet it assists you when you need it most. Starting at 1st level, whenever you hit with a weapon attack using your imbued weapon, note the result of the attack roll and your Imbuement die roll.

*Odd.* If the result of the attack roll is odd, you gain temporary hit points equal to the result of your Imbuement die, which disappears whenever you take a short or long rest.

*Even.* If the result of the attack roll is even, the attack deals extra damage equal to the result of your Imbuement die.

## 6th Level - Volatile Surge

The unpredictable effects of your magic have branched into even more peculiar effects. Starting at 6th level, when you cast a channeler spell, roll your Elemental Imbuement die and note the result. Consult the Volatile Surge table for additional effects.

##### Surge Effect

| Result | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | You take damage equal to one roll of your Elemental Imbuement die. |
| 2 | You add another roll of your Elemental Imbuement die to the spell’s damage. |
| 3 | Your AC increases by 1 until the start of your next turn. |
| 4 | You gain advantage on your next attack roll until the end of your next turn. |
| 5 | You gain additional temporary hit points equal to one roll of your Elemental Imbuement die. |
| 6 | You can make an additional weapon attack as part of the same bonus action. |
| 7 | You gain resistance to the next attack that hits you until the start of your next turn. |
| 8 | Reroll on this table twice, and the effects of both rolls occur. If you roll the same effect twice, the effect only applies once. |

## 11th Level - Risk and Reward

At 11th level; your frequent interactions with fickle energies have allowed you to lean further into their volatility. You learn one additional Ante option, and gain an additional Ante charge.

## 11th Level - Extra Attack

Beginning at 11th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn.

## 17th Level - Culmination of Volatility

At 17th level, your destructive magic is longing for escape from your mortal shell. Once per day, when you’re reduced to zero hit points, you may choose to release all of your internal energy in one burst. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you must roll a dexterity saving throw equal to your spell save DC. On a failed save, a target takes 15d10 damage and is stunned until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and isn’t stunned. The damage type is the same as your elemental Imbuement. In either case, the explosion kills you immediately, disintegrating all non-magical gear and equipment you’re carrying and wearing.

items.name: Channeler's Clay
items.description: This is a glob of discolored clay that you apply to a weapon you're proficient with. While wielding the applied weapon, you gain a bonus to spell attack rolls and to saving throw DCs of your channeler spells. This bonus is determined by the clay's rarity. In addition, you can regain 1 ante as an action while wielding the applied weapon. You can't use this property again until you finish a long rest.

items.name: Claw caps
items.description:
*5gp, 1 lbs. --- 1d4 Slashing - Finesse, Light, Nick*

**Finesse:** When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

**Light:** A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

**Special:** Claw Caps can be fitted over a creature's natural weapons as an action changing it's base statistics and properties to match that of the natural weapon it is fitted over, it then gains a new weapon mastery depending on the natural weapon's damage type:

*   Bludgeoning: Topple
*   Piercing: Sap
*   Slashing: Vex

*Natural Weapons with Claw Caps cannot be used to make unarmed strikes.*

items.name: Clawshot
items.description:
This magical item is made up of two distinct parts, the first being a claw-like structure, and the second being a handle. The two pieces fit together in such a way that the open claw points away from your hand when held.

While you are holding the handle, and the claw is attached, you can use a bonus action to speak the items command word, causing the claw to be magically propelled up to 30 feet. When you do so, you may target either an object or creature. At the end of the claws journey, the claw embeds itself harmlessly into the target and two pieces are magically pulled back together. If the target object or creature is either your size or larger, you are pulled up to 30 feet towards it. Inversely, if the target object or creature is at least one size smaller than you, it is pulled up to 30 feet towards you.

If the target is a creature, make an attack roll with a +10 bonus against its armor class. If the attack misses, the claw returns to the handle without moving either you or the target. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks.

The clawshot is a magical device first invented by the artificer Flare. The clawshot is used for quick movement and repositioning, and for getting past otherwise impassable obstacles. It is also quite effective in low gravity environments, like those it's inventor commonly reside.

> *"It's perfectly safe to use, I swear. Just look at me, I use it all the time!" - Flare*

items.name: Cloak of Levitation
items.description:
The Cloak of Levitation is one of many items imbued with power to sustain magics that practitioners of the mystic arts cannot. While you wear this cloak, you have a fly speed equal to your walking speed. If you fall while in the air when wearing this cloak, you descend at a rate of 30 feet per round until you land, taking no fall damage. If you are not wearing armor, your base Armor Class is 13 + your Dexterity modifier while wearing this cloak. When a melee attack made against you would reduce you to 20 points or lower, you can use your reaction to have the cloak fly off of your shoulders and immediately attempt a grapple against the attacker. The cloak counts as a medium sized creature for the purposes of grappling and has a +8 modifier to Strength (Athletics) checks made to grapple. If the the grapple is successful the attacker also gains the Blinded condition for the duration of the grapple. As a bonus action you may call the cloak back to you if it is within 60 feet.

When targeted by an attack or by a feature that forces a Dexterity saving throw, you may use your reaction to have the cloak take the brunt of the attack, transferring half of the damage you take to the cloak. Once the cloak is used this way, it cannot be used to fly until the next dawn. Additionally, this property cannot be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Cloud in a Bottle
items.description: A swirling cloud sealed within a small glass bottle. While a Cloud in a Bottle is on your person, you gain the ability to jump while mid-air. Additionally, you can treat your long and high jumps as if you had moved 10 feet before doing so, even if you didn't. Once you jump in this way, you cannot do so again until you land on the ground.

items.name: Cold Cube
items.description: This 3-inch cube is the direct counterpart of the thermal cube, it emanates cold to keep the temperature within 15 ft of it at 41 degrees Fahrenheit (5 degrees Celsius)

items.name: Cold Hypercube
items.description: This 3-inch cube is a direct counterpart of the Thermal Hypercube. The cube resonates slightly when touched, and emanates cold to keep the temperature within 30 ft of it at -20 degrees Fahrenheit (~-29 degrees Celcius). This area is also subject to extreme cold.

items.name: Communication Mouthpiece
items.description: This mask like accessories produces a voice based on the wearers thoughts, it fits over the mouth and allows someone or something who doesn't have capabilities of speech to speak a language they know, in addition, you are able to speak and understand spoken Common while wearing this mask

items.name: Consecrated Scabbard
items.description:
This scabbard is decorated with depictions of the deity it was dedicated to, and it automatically adjusts to what kind of melee weapon it will hold, such as changing size to fit any blade or becoming a series of metal rings to holster weapons like axes, hammers, or spears. When you draw a melee weapon that is held by this scabbard, you can bless the weapon. For the next minute, whenever you make an attack roll with this weapon, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the attack roll. Additionally, attacks made with this melee weapon are considered magical and deal an additional 1d4 radiant damage. This effect ends early if you stow the weapon or if a different creature other than you wields it. The effect can be applied three times, after which you regain all expended uses at the next dawn.

If you are under the effects of the bless spell while wielding a melee weapon with the scabbard's effect, the d4s granted by the bless spell and this item turn into d6s.

items.name: Conspirator's Coat
items.description:
This magic longcoat is adorned with raven feathers and carved jet buttons. While wearing this coat, you have advantage on Charisma (Deception) checks. Inaddition, the coat has 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges daily at dusk. While wearing the coat, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 of its charges to disappear, releasing up to three ravens from your location under your control that scatters into the air. Each raven has AC 12, 1 hit point, and can fly up to 40 feet as part of this bonus action without provoking opportunity attacks. At the start of your next turn, the ravens vanish, and you reappear in the space of one of the ravens (your choice).

If all the ravens are reduced to 0 hit points, you reappear early in the same space as the last one that died. When you do, any excess damage dealt to the raven carries over to your normal form.

items.name: Container of Heat and Frost
items.description:
This dense metal container is adorned with three arcane designs along the edge of its removable lid. When filled with up to one gallon of watery liquid (Liquid with the same viscosity as water), the vessel can either heat, chill, or maintain the liquid's temperature. As an action, you can press one of the arcane symbols to heat the liquid to a boil (212 degrees Fahrenheit or 100 degrees Celsius), chill it (33 degrees Fahrenheit or 1 degree Celsius), or keep it at temperature for up to 24 hours.

It takes one minute for the vessel to heat or cool a liquid down, and, once at temperature, maintains it. Liquids placed inside without activating the arcane designs will maintain its temperature for up to one hour before becoming tepid.

items.name: Corrosion Cube
items.description:
This 3' cube glows slightly with yellow light while active, which can be identified as a standard "hazard" symbol while in darkness.

The cube has 3 charges. As an action, you can touch the cube to a non-magical metallic object. If the object is smaller than the cube, it is destroyed as the object melts over the course of one minute. Otherwise, the cube melts away a section of the object equal to the cube in volume over the course of one minute. Either result spends one charge of the cube. The cube regains all of it's charges daily at dawn.

The cube makes a fairly loud cracking sound as it melts metal, which can be heard easily from up to 30ft away.

items.name: Courier's Cradle
items.description:
*A Couriers Cradle needs two pieces to have any sort of effect. A Sending Stone, and a Couriers Cradle. Without both components it has no function.* A Courier's Cradle consists of three parts. A chassis, typically squared and made of polished stone, granite, or marble for particularly wealthy folk. A basin sat in the cubed bottom, around half a foot wide and typically filled with water. And lastly a bowl mounted over the basin, made of rare material such as gemstone, brass, or pure quartz. While a creature is within 5 feet of a cradle, it may place a sending stone into it's bowl. It then takes a minute to fasten and adjust the cradle for speaking. After this process is complete the cradle is assembled.

Connection A creature may connect to another Courier's Cradle within a distance of up to the plane they exist according to the following difficulties, using an Intelligence (Arcana) check. If another Cradle is connected to this one, they establish a line of communication. This allows each to hear from the other side as if they were in the same room.



| Distance (Miles) | DC |
| --- | --- |
| 5 | 10 |
| 50 | 15 |
| 200 | 20 |
| Planar | 25 |

items.name: Craorag Dromlach
items.description:
*The spear once used by the Storm Maiden of Norland, Hergatha, was said to be lost following her ultimate defeat during her efforts to conquer the Sea of Swords. It was written to have been bestowed by Tempus, but rumors whisper that it was actually bestowed to her by Valkur.*

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to piercing damage.

items.name: Crown of the Four Seasons
items.description:
*Control your emotions, sweetheart, or the witches will come and steal your feelings away.*

Originally used as a toy to train young eladrin, this fey-infused flower crown changes appearance based on the wearer's mood. These moods also reflect the four seasons: winter, spring, summer, and autumn.

The flowers will shift between four seasonal types dependent on the wearer's mood and the corresponding season. In addition, a harmless illusory effect will hover over the blossoms to reflect the same thing (Snowflakes fluttering around for winter/sadness, Fiery sparks for summer/passion etc).

When the wearer undergoes a burst of any type of emotion (Flash of anger, sudden surprise etc), the flowers will wilt to a dull gray and the illusory effects will vanish to signify a loss of control. The next morning, the flowers will blossom to their full color after the lesson has been learned

items.name: Cryomancy
items.description:
## Frigid Magic

Your manipulation of ice allow you to acclimate to it and change the properties of spells. When you cast a spell that deals damage, you are able to change the damage type to cold damage unless its bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

You gain resistance to cold damage. When you reach 13th level on this class, you also gain resistance to fire damage, and at 17th level, you gain immunity to cold damage and the area 5 feet of you has the effects of extreme cold.

## Ice Sculpting

As an action, you can freeze a 5-foot cube of nonmagical water. The cube will remain frozen for one minute or until your concentration ends (as if you were concentrating on a spell). You can manipulate the ice as a bonus action to mold its shape to form objects or structures. The objects or structures you make have hit points equal to your wizard level. At 8th and 15th level, you freeze an area of 15-foot and 25-foot cube respectively. You can also create the ice out of moisture in the air, though the cubic footage you are able to manipulate is halved (i.e. you can produce a 7-foot cube from a 15-foot cube of air).

Additionally, when you reach 15th level you can also freeze magical water, manipulate magical ice, and the cube can now be frozen for up to 1 hour.

## Cryostasis

Your body fills with ice magic and acts at times in preservation from external factors. When you become unconscious or asleep or are rendered unconscious or asleep, you are immediately encased in a layer of dense ice. This ice has hit points equal to twice your wizard level and an AC equal to your spell save DC.

When the ice encases you as a result of being reduced to 0 hit points, the ice will stabilize you and freezes the area around you. All creatures within 10 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or be restrained until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, they are not restrained but their speed is halved. When the restrained condition from this feature ends, the creature's speed is also halved. They can make another Constitution saving throw at the end of each of their turns. On a success, their speed returns to normal.

The ice lasts for 1 minute, until it is destroyed, or when you choose to break the ice upon regaining consciousness. Once this feature is used it cannot be used again until a long rest.

## Absolute Zero

Your control over ice have reached to an abnormal extreme, even by magical standards. Choose one wizard spell that deals damage in your spellbook. That spell's damage type permanently becomes cold damage when you cast it without using your Frigid Magic feature or other similar effect. You can change the spell for a different spell by spending 8 hours in study.

The first time you deal damage to a creature that does not have resistance or immunity to cold damage on your turn, they take extra cold damage equal to your wizard level.

## Whiteout

After extensive use of bending ice to your every whim, you understand all of its inner workings. You're able to apply this knowledge by performing grand feats on a monumental scale. As an action, you are able to freeze everything within a 60 foot radius sphere centered around you. This creates sleets of ice and snow on the ground and super chilling the air, rendering the area as difficult terrain. All creatures of your choice takes 3d6 cold damage upon activation and must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or be frozen. While frozen, a creature is considered incapacitated and their speed is reduced to 0 until the end of their next turn.

Furthermore, you create a 30 foot radius sphere of a perpetual blizzard that is centered around and moves with you. When a creature of your choice enters the area for the first time or starts their turn in it, the creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or take cold damage equal to h…

items.name: Darkweavers Mantle
items.description: While you are in an area of dim light or darkness, you can Hide as a bonus action if you are not being directly observed. The cloak has 3 charges for the following other properties. The cloak regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

items.name: Dawnbreak Pearl
items.description: Used to summon daylight, this 6-inch-diameter, gold-white orb is warm to the touch. You can spend 10 minutes to activate it, causing the area within 10 miles of it at the moment of activation to become daylight even if it is nighttime. This daylight lasts for 24 hours, until you cancel it as an action, or until your attunement to the pearl ends. Once used, the pearl can't be used again for 24 hours.

items.name: Designated Mage's Rifle
items.description: This magical firearm is designed for use in magical combat. Due to this fact, a spellcaster can use the weapon as their spellcasting focus. You gain a +1/2/3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magical weapons, and the same bonus to spell attack rolls you make while holding this weapon. When you cast a spell using this weapon as your spellcasting focus, your next attack made with the weapon uses your spellcasting ability instead of dexterity. Additionally, if the spell deals damage, the weapon's damage type changes to that of the spell.

items.name: Double-Bladed Scythe
items.description: Proficiency with a double-bladed scythe allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

items.name: Dragon Fang Gun
items.description:
The barrel of this magic firearm is shaped like a dragon's maw, and the weapon is infused with the essence of a chromatic, gem, or metallic dragon's breath. When you hit with an attack roll using this magic firearm, the target takes an extra 1d6 damage of the same type as the breath infused in the firearm--acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, necrotic, poison, psychic, radiant, or thunder.

If you load no ammunition in the weapon, it produces its own, automatically creating one piece of magic ammunition when you pull the trigger. The ammunition created by the firearm vanishes the instant after it hits or misses a target.

items.name: Dragonscale Knuckles
items.description:
While wearing these knuckles you get a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with unarmed strikes. While these knuckles are equipped you can’t use other weapons and the knuckles take an action to equip or unequip. The knuckles deal an additional 1d6 damage of which the type is determined by the dragon scales used to create it.



| Scale Color | Damage Type |
| --- | --- |
| Red | Fire |
| Green | Poison |
| Blue | Lightning |
| Black | Acid |
| White | Cold |
| Gold | Fire |
| Brass | Fire |
| Copper | Acid |
| Silver | Cold |
| Bronze | Lightning |
| Sapphire | Thunder |
| Emerald | Psychic |
| Amethyst | Force |
| Crystal | Radiant |
| Topaz | Necrotic |

items.name: Drow Shadow Blade
items.description:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You also add 1d4 to all stealth checks made while you are holding this weapon.

This dagger appears to be of Elven make, and has a long, cruel, slender black blade. The dagger always appears wreathed in shadows, making it hard to focus upon clearly, even on a bright summer's day. It also bestows some of this uncanny ability to its wielder.

Proficiency with a Dagger allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it

items.name: Duality Blade
items.description:
One Blade of Light contains the essence of goodness and holiness, the other Blade of Darkness contains the essence of evil and deceit.

When attacking with this weapon you choose with which blade you attack.

items.name: Dugger's Tome
items.description:
A Dugger's Tome is a large leather bound book. Each tome can only be used once by placing it on any book, as soon as it touches the other book, the Tome will become an exact replica of the copied book, and the copied book now has a magical aura of conjuration magic, whatever happens to one book will likewise happen to the other if both are on the same plane (Words written, water spilled, pages torn etc)

It was originally created to sync libraries and records across long distances but was later adapted by spies and criminal for espionage.

items.name: Elemental Weapon
items.description:
You have a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon and on a hit, the weapon deals an extra 1d6 damage of a type determined by the runes engraved upon the weapon, which the DM chooses or determines randomly.



| D5 | Extra Damage Type |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Fire |
| 2 | Cold |
| 3 | Lightning |
| 4 | Thunder |
| 5 | Acid |

items.name: Endless Flask of Hot Cocoa
items.description:
A simple, platinum-plated square flask carved with simple, almost childish sylvan runes. You cannot fill this flask with anything, as it is perpetually filled with fresh hot cocoa, peppered with little marshmallows, and partially melted chocolate chips.

Sprinkling a pinch of flavorings, such as caramel sugars or peppermint crumbs, will permanently flavor the hot cocoa similarly. This can be reversed by dropping in a pinch of unflavored chocolate chips.

If you pour hot chocolate from the bottle, rather than drinking from it, the liquid vanishes the moment it leaves the bottle.

items.name: Enduring Scabbard
items.description: While a sword is within the scabbard, the blade can't be damaged by fire or rusted by water. In addition, the scabbard preserves the current sharpness of the blade ensuring it never dulls while sheathed.

items.name: Eye of the Bookworm
items.description:
This small, glass sphere is enchanted with weak divination magics to help you find specific words or phrases in writing. It has a stylized iris etched into the glass.

You can use a bonus action to speak the Eye's command word aloud, followed by the word or phrase you're searching for. The word or phrase magically appears and floats within the sphere. By holding the Eye in a free hand, your vision becomes magically enhanced to subconsciously highlight the word or phrase you searched for in a green light whenever you see it. Once you've used the Eye's ability for the first time, you can change the word or phrase as many times as you like for the next hour, after which the item can not be used again until the following dawn.

items.name: Faceless Cloak
items.description: There are several known versions of this hooded cloak; some look plain and common, while others appear intricate and expensive. When the hood is raised, a minor illusion appears which hides the wearer's face in total magical darkness. The wearer can see out with no penalties, but only truesight or other magical means of seeing through illusions can penetrate the hood's darkness from outside.

items.name: Failnaught
items.description:
This arrow has a black lusterous shaft and adorned by an arrowhead made from a crystal like material that shines a gentle blue hue.

In its original form, this magic arrow is ammuntion for bows but can alter its form(no action required) into a bolt that can be loaded to crossbows. The Failnaught never misses on a ranged attack and is an automatic hit, it will curve and move on its own in mid-air until it hits its target. If no open pathway is available, it pierces through any physical obstruction that is less than 1 foot thick or any magical barrier that may block its path (i.e. a metal door or a wall of force spell). The Failnaught uses a damage die of 1d8 instead of the weapon's damage die and ignores any damage resistances and immunities that it deals or inflicts. It retains its magical properties after an attack and can be reused again.

Alternatively, the Failnaught can be used without a bow or crossbow, it can be used as a dart with a damage die of 1d6.

\[When obtained in any way, quest, loot, craft(if CMs make it craftable), etc., you only get 1 arrow and not a bundle.\]

items.name: Fallen King's Halberd
items.description: This halberd was owned by a great king whose name is long forgotten who reigned from a kingdom that has long been destroyed. Its old but strangely robust body has scattered dents and damage from all around. In contrast, both the point and blade remain seamless and sharp against all the roughness of use and wear it has experienced.

items.name: Fey Duelist
items.description:
## Description

The Fey Duelist archetype merges the cunning and agility of a rogue with the enchanting power of the Feywild. These rogues are gifted (or cursed) with the boons of Archfey patrons, weaving magic and mischief into their every movement. They are agile combatants and masters of manipulation, channeling Fey magic to outmaneuver foes, charm allies, and spread chaos on the battlefield.

Whether as emissaries of a Fey court, champions of their Archfey patron, or independent agents who stumbled into the gifts of the Feywild, Fey Duelists tread a fine line between mortal pragmatism and the capricious beauty of the Fey. Their motivations are as varied as the Feywild itself, often driven by a blend of personal ambition, devotion to their patron, or a desire to unweave the chains of mundane reality and reshape the world with Fey-inspired artistry.

#### Playstyle and Motivations

The Fey Duelist excels in roles that combine agility, cunning, and creativity.
