They bring versatility to combat and social interactions, offering a mix of mobility, control, and spellcasting that allows them to adapt to nearly any situation. Players who enjoy weaving spells and martial combat together will find the Fey Duelist a rewarding choice.

Motivations for a Fey Duelist might include:

: Serving a powerful Fey entity as a trusted agent or champion, spreading their influence or carrying out their will.
*   **Personal Freedom**: Using Fey gifts to escape or defy systems of control, embracing the chaotic and whimsical nature of the Feywild.
*   **Vengeance or Redemption**: Seeking to right a past wrong or balance a perceived injustice using the unique powers of the Fey.
*   **Curiosity and Mischief**: Exploring the boundaries of mortal and Fey realms, motivated by wonder and the joy of discovery.

#### Who Would Thrive as a Fey Duelist?

The Fey Duelist appeals to characters who want to:

*   **Command the Social Arena**: Features like *Binding Words* provide robust tools for persuasion, manipulation, and intrigue.
*   **Outmaneuver and Disrupt Enemies**: Abilities like *Elusive Action* and *Distant Dodge* emphasize mobility and tactical positioning.

#### Ideal Players and Characters

*   **The Charismatic Manipulator**: Players who enjoy talking their way out of trouble, influencing NPCs, and using charm to solve problems.
*   **The Tactical Trickster**: Players who like to use cunning, positioning, and the environment to control the flow of combat.
*   **The Fey Enthusiast**: Players who love the whimsical, mysterious, and chaotic themes of the Feywild and want to embody its spirit.

**Features**

*   [3rd Level - Spellcasting](#3rd-level---spellcasting)
*   [3rd Level - Vexing](#3rd-level---vexing)
*   [9th Level - Delicate Deception](#9th-level---delicate-deception)
*   [13th Level - Binding Words](#13th-level---binding-words)
*   [17th Level - Archfeys Guile](#17th-level---archfeys-guile)

items.name: Fighter's Band
items.description:
This finely detailed band can be a ring, bracelet, necklace or anklet. While wearing it, you gain a bonus to the saving throw DCs of your fighter features. The bonus is determined by the band's rarity.

In addition, when you use your *Second Wind* feature, you can regain additional hit points equal to 1d10 + half your fighter level. You can't use this feature again until the next dawn.

items.name: Final Messenger
items.description:
Wondrous item, uncommon (requires use by a warforged)

A final messenger is a tiny homunculus—a delicate winged creature designed to facilitate communication between warforged soldiers during the Last War.If you are a warforged, you can use an action to hold a final messenger and integrate it into your body. While it is part of your body, the messenger cannot be targeted by any attack. You can only have one messenger attached to your body at a time.

A final messenger holds four pieces of data, accessible only to the warforged it is attached to:

*   A destination for the messenger. This can be very specific (a particular apartment in Sharn) or more general (an open marketplace).
*   A target individual. This could be a specific person or a category (“Any Karrnathi warforged”).
*   A single image recorded through the eyes of a warforged attached to the messenger.
*   A message of up to 25 words.

While the messenger is attached to you, you can use an action to review the data currently stored within the final messenger, and if you choose, to change any or all of these data points. You can release the final messenger as a bonus action, at which point it flies to its destination by the most direct route possible. Once it reaches the destination it seeks the target individual; if no target has been set, it approaches the nearest warforged.

If you are reduced to 0 hit points while a final messenger is attached to you, it immediately detaches and seek out its target. It retains its stored message, but replaces its stored image with the last thing you saw before dropping to zero hit points.

A final messenger is a Tiny construct with an AC of 20, 6 hit points, and a flying speed of 60 feet. It is immune to poison damage, psychic damage, and all conditions other than unconsciousness. If it is forced to make an ability check or a saving throw, treat all its ability scores as 10 (+0). It has darkvision with a range of 60 feet. It has no limbs and the only action it can take on its turn is the Dash action. A messenger can only remain active for 8 hours after it detaches from a warforged body, after which time its energy dissipates and it falls unconscious. An unconscious messenger must complete a short or long rest attached to a warforged, after which it regains its energy, as well as regaining all lost hit points.

items.name: Flame Cartridge
items.description:
A selection of various flammable substances combined with a hint of evocation magic allows this specialty ammunition to turn any firearm into a different style of weapon.

Weapons loaded with a flame cartridge deal fire damage instead of the damage type it would normally deal. Additionally, when you attack with this weapon, instead of making an attack roll, the target must succeed a dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + the weapon's attack bonus) or take the weapon's damage, or half as much on a successful save.

Effects that require you to hit a target instead require them to fail their saving throw.

items.name: Flameburst Devastator
items.description:
This heavy incendiary weapon functions by superheating the metal scraps typically used as ammunition by a scrapshot, resulting in a spread of molten slag being launched at its targets.

This magical weapon has a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls, and deals fire damage instead of slashing. Targets hit by the weapon are coated in molten metal until they, or a creature within 5 ft of them, use an action to scrape it off of them. A creature or object coated in this molten metal takes 1d10 fire damage at the start of each of its turns. The molten metal effect can stack up to three times.

Additionally, creatures reduced to 0 hitpoints by damage from this weapon are reduced to ash.

items.name: Fortune's Shadow
items.description:
## Fortune's Gambit

At 3rd level, upon dealing sneak attack damage with an even number, you gain a Fate Point.

As a bonus action, you can expend a Fate Point to make a daring gambit. Roll a d6 to determine the effect of the Gambit.

d6 : Gambit : Effect:

1.  **Fleeting Shadows:** *You become invisible until the start of your next turn, you lose invisibility whenever you attack, or force a saving throw.*
2.  **Lucky Strike:** *Your next attack gains advantage.*
3.  **Evasive Maneuver:** *The next attack against you must be made with disadvantage.*
4.  **Weave of Fate:** *You gain temporary hit points equal to your rogue level.*
5.  **Deft Diversion:** *You can move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks.*
6.  **Fortune's Grace:** *Add your proficiency bonus to your AC until the start of your next turn.*

When rolling initiative your Fate Points reset to 0. You can a flip a coin, on a head to you gain a Fate point.

## Fateful Escape

At level 9 when you are reduced to 0 hit points and not killed outright, you can expend a Fate Point to cheat death. Instead of falling unconscious, you remain conscious with 1 hit point, and can immediately use a reaction to move up to your speed, without provoking opportunity attacks. Additionally, you gain advantage on Dexterity ability checks and saving throws for 1 minute, representing the temporary escape from fate’s grasp. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

## Greater Gambit

At 13th level your Gambit's are improved. when you roll for a Gambit, you instead use the Greater gambit table:

d6 : Gambit : Effect:

1.  ***Persistent Shadows*:** *You become invisible until the end of your next turn.*
2.  ***Foretold Strikes*:** *You gain advantage on attacks until the end of your next turn.*
3.  ***Evasive Expert*:** *You impose disadvantage on all attacks against you until the start of your next turn..*
4.  ***Architect of Fate*:** *You gain temporary hit points and regain hit point equal to your rogue level*
5.  ***Perfect Diversion*:** *You can move up to your speed without provoking opportunity attacks.*
6.  ***Fortune's Gift*:** *Add your proficiency bonus to your AC until the start of your next turn. Reduce damage taken from attacks by the same amount.*

When rolling initiative you can now flip 2 coins, gaining a Fate point for each coin that lands on a head.

## Uncanny Fate

At level 17, when you use your uncanny dodge feature to halve damage received, you can spend a Fate Point to roll a d6. On an even result, the attacker takes force damage equal to the damage you received. On an odd result, you don't take any damage from the attack.

items.name: Frostflames of the North
items.description:
The torch, whose wood carry no magical properties, brings an icy blue flame that defied the very essence of warmth but still possess its comfort and reliability. This torch provides bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet much like normal torches except this is unimpeded by effects caused by cold environments and instead overcomes them (i.e., instead of being subjected to a heavily obscuring blizzard, you and those around you are able to see with ease within this torch's light). Creatures within its light's range are free from the effects of extreme heat. If you make a melee attack with this lit torch and hit, it deals 1 cold damage. If the flames are in contact to a body of water, it will slowly freeze the water within its light's range.


You can freely decrease the size of the flame and the light it makes to an inch in diameter and revert it back, but it cannot be completely snuffed out. You can transfer it to another torch and leave the previous holder as a mundane torch.

If this torch is held by a creature that does not have resistance or immunity to cold damage, they will take 1 cold damage every minute until they drop this torch.

The blue flame has 6 charges. While holding the torch, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to blow on the fires to cast the cone of cold spell (save DC 17; you can choose whether each creature makes a Constitution or Dexterity saving throw) from it. Each expended charge makes the flames smaller and smaller. If you expend the last charge, the flames and light it produces will only be 1 inch in diameter and will stay in that size. The blue flame regains 1 charge daily at dawn, as well as increase in size bit by bit.

items.name: Gambler's Weapon
items.description: Choose a magical bonus of +1 to +3. This weapon gains that bonus to its attack and damage rolls. For each point of bonus you choose for the weapon, you take a corresponding penalty (−1 to −3) to your death saving throws. You can change this magical bonus each day at dawn.

items.name: Gauntlets Of Gunslinging
items.description: While wearing these gauntlets, you have proficiency with the musket and pistol, and you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls on ranged attacks made with such weapons.

items.name: Gift of the Upper Planes
items.description:
You manifested some of the powers from the Upper Planes, granting you the following benefits:

*   Your Healing Hands and transformation traits can now use either an action or bonus action to activate and can be used again after finishing a short or long rest.
*   Each of your transformations also gain additional effects:
    *

items.name: Glider Staff
items.description:
As a reaction or an object interaction, this quarterstaff can be converted into a glider, or from a glider back into the quarterstaff. It can't be used as a weapon while in glider mode. As a bonus action you can will the glider to activate.

For 1 hour you have a flying speed of 45 feet, but you can't use this speed to gain altitude, and while using this speed you lose 5 feet of altitude plus any unused lateral movement at the end of each turns. (For example if you fly 30 feet on a turn, you will fall 20 feet at the end of the turn. You can land safely this way.)

items.name: Gloves of the Spirit Shroud
items.description: These gloves have 3 charges. While wearing them, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast the spell Spirit Shroud from it. For 1 charge, you cast the 3rd-level version of the spell. You can increase the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend. The gloves regain all expended charges daily at dawn.

items.name: Greater Healing Kit
items.description:
This kit is made from a combination of rare herbs and magical compounds, providing potent healing properties.

Special bandages infused with healing magic, designed to accelerate the natural healing process.

items.name: Greater Ritual Casting
items.description:
Your mastery of ritual magic reaches new heights. You gain the following benefits:

    •    **Ability Score Increase.** Increase the same ability score you chose for the Ritual Caster feat (Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma) by 1, to a maximum of 20.

    •    **Quick Ritual**. Once more per long rest, you can cast a ritual spell at its normal casting time instead of adding 10 minutes.

    •    **Expanded Ritual Repertoire**. You can learn one additional ritual spell of each level equal to or lower than your spellcasting ability modifier(maximum 5).

    •    **Growth of Knowledge**. For each level you gain, you may replace one ritual spell of that level with another once on a level up, or when you PERMANENTLY increase your casting modifier.

items.name: Guardian Angel
items.description:
This shield is adorned with angelic wings and a crystal at the center which glows with a calming blue hue. While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC and resistance to necrotic damage.

When you die as a result of failing all your death saving throws or when you are subjected to an effect which causes you to instantly die, you instead fall into a stasis until the start of your next turn. During this stasis, you are incapacitated while remain conscious of your surroundings, you are immune to all damage and cannot be affected by any other effect or condition. If you are under other effects or conditions when you fall into stasis, all of these instantly end. At the start of your next turn, your current hit point total resets to 20 hit points and you gain advantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw you make. Once you have used this feature, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Guild Palace Lance
items.description:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Attacking a target within 5 feet of you with this weapon doesn't impose disadvantage on the attack roll.

If you are attuned to Guild Palace Shield, you gain access to the following additional properties:

*   The bonus to attack and damage rolls instead becomes +3.
*   You can wield this weapon with one hand, instead of two, while also holding the shield.
*   When you attack with this weapon, you deal an additional 1d12 force damage. If you roll a 20 on the attack roll, it rolls damage dice three times, instead of twice.

You can cast the Raulothim's Psychic Lance spell (save DC 17). Once you cast the spell this way, you regain the ability to use it again at dawn.

items.name: Guild Palace Shield
items.description:
While holding this shield, you have a +2 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC. If you are attuned to Guild Palace Lance, you gain access to the following additional properties:

*   Your attack rolls using the Guild Palace Lance have advantage against targets that is 10 feet away from you.
*   The bonus to AC instead becomes +3.
*   The first time an attacker misses you with a melee attack in a turn, they take 1d8 force damage.
*   You can cast the Globe of Invulnerability spell. Once you cast the spell this way, you regain the ability to use it again at dawn.

items.name: Hammer of the Ram
items.description:
This all-silver hammer carries a mighty force behind it with every swing.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

If the hammer hits an object that isn’t being worn or carried, the hit is a critical hit.

The hammer has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a target with the hammer, you can expend 1 to 3 of its charges to force the target to make a DC 15 Strength saving throw. For each charge expended, the target takes an extra 1d10 force damage and is pushed 5 feet away from you on a failed save. On a successful save, the target takes half as much force damage and isn’t pushed.

𝙍𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙤𝙛 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙍𝙖𝙢. If you’re also attuned to the “ring of the ram,” your bonus to attack and damage rolls with the hammer increases to +2, and the hammer’s every swing creates a projection of the spectral ram from the ring.

The ram lurches forward from the head of the hammer, effectively increasing your reach with the weapon by an additional 5 feet. In addition, the hammer and ring share a pool of charges: any charge you have with the ring can be used for the hammer’s properties, and vice versa.

items.name: Healing bow
items.description: This bow has 6 charges and regains 1d4+2 charges at dawn. When you use your action to fire this bow, you can expend a number of charges to shoot one of the arrows at an ally within normal range. This arrow deals no damage, but restores 1d6 HP to the target for each charge expended. Alternatively, once per turn, you may instead use one of your attacks to fire a single arrow at an ally within the normal range of the bow. When you do so, you Cast a Spell you have prepared that restores HP to a single target other than the caster, does not deal damage, and has a casting time of 1 action. The arrow deals no damage, but the effects of the spell are applied to the target. Casting a spell in this way does not require a free hand. If the spell requires a spell slot, the slot is expended as usual.

items.name: Heartseeker Bow
items.description: A Heartseeker bow is a beautifully crafted elven longbow +3 made of polished wood. The elven craftsmen made the bow so strong even stone cannot scratch the marvelous wood, and the deep finish of its polish can be returned by a single brush of the hand. You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, in addition to the following abilities.

items.name: Homebrew System & Index
items.description:
## How to Homebrew

In The Wandering Realms, we allow homebrew. Homebrew get approved in the server on a dual voting system. Any Player can submit homebrew in the #homebrew-ua-submissions Channel. Each homebrew submission follows the same method:

1.  A Player Creates a Post in the #homebrew-ua-submissions channel. They Ping @homebrewer in the post. And react on the post with a 1, 2 and 3.
2.  Players then vote on the submission by reacting.
    1.  Yes
    2.  No
    3.  Abstain
3.  Once the Submission reaches 10 Yes votes, the submission is moved to homebrew-ua-approver-votes where a team of staff members will either approve or deny the homebrew

Some Rules:

1.  A Player is allowed one submission per week. Unless their submission get's approved by staff, in which case they can submit another.
2.  #homebrew-ua-submissions is reserved for submissions only. Players can discuss or query active homebrew in #homebrew-ua-discussion.


When submitting Homebrew, a player can declare an item to be invented, or otherwise created by one or multiple characters. The creation or access to that Homebrew is then restricted to those with the same in-character access to it. In order to maintain this restriction, the actual creation must be roleplayed within a reasonable timeframe of the item's passing (no people rushing to craft an item the minute it passes before a person can rp)

ie: a wizard sharing a spell they created with another; an inventor selling items or sharing designs

items.name: Impact Cannon
items.description:
This modified blunderbuss instead condenses all of its ammunition into a single clump, which detonates on impact.

This weapon deals bludgeoning damage instead of piercing damage. When you hit a target with this weapon, it and every other creature within 5 ft of the target must succeed a DC15 dexterity saving throw, taking 2d6 slashing damage on a failed save.

items.name: Iron Heart
items.description: Made as a replacement for a faulty heart, this metallic organ was created to help terminally ill patients become well again. To use this item, you need to undergo an hour-long ritual in which the heart merges into your chest, permanently replacing your original heart. As well as being able to function as a normal heart, this magic item grants you advantage on any saving throws or checks made to resist or end blood-borne diseases and injury poisons.

items.name: Kusarigama
items.description: **Special

items.name: Last Stand Handgun
items.description:
This pistol feeds off of its users vitality, but can provide great assistance in their hour of need.


You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this magic weapon. Every time you attack with this weapon, you take 2 necrotic damage that cannot be reduced in any way. Additionally while you are below 25 hit points, all targets are vulnerable to the piercing damage dealt by this weapon.


If you are reduced to 0 hit points or die from causes other than this weapon's self damage, you are revived with 1 hit point at the start of your next turn. During this turn, the weapon does not require your hit points to attack, but you die at the end of your turn. Once the weapon has revived you, it cannot do so again until the next dawn.

items.name: Lesser Sun Blade
items.description:
This item appears to be a longsword hilt. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause a blade of pure radiance to spring into existence, or make the blade disappear. While the blade exists, this magic longsword has the finesse property. If you are proficient with short-swords or longswords, you are proficient with the lesser sun blade.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon, which deals radiant damage instead of slashing damage. When you hit an undead with it, that target takes an extra 1d4 radiant damage.

The sword's luminous blade emits bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. The light is sunlight. While the blade persists, you can use an action to expand or reduce its radius of bright and dim light by 5 feet each, to a maximum of 30 feet each or a minimum of 5 feet each.

items.name: Lesser Thought Gem
items.description: While holding the lesser thought gem, you can use an action to store a single thought strand created by the encode thoughts cantrip. The thought strand remains present for seven days before it decays, vanishing from the gem. You can use an action to touch the gem's surface and retrieve a thought strand within it, though it maintains its original duration as well as the decay time from being stored. Only one thought strand can be stored in a lesser thought gem at a time.

items.name: Light Weaver
items.description:
## Description


Features:

[1st Level - Trick of the Light](#1st-level---trick-of-the-light)

[1st Level - Flickering Aura](#1st-level---flickering-aura)

[6th Level - Refraction Shield](#6th-level---refraction-shield)

[14th Level - Aura Magnification](#14th-level---aura-magnification)

[18th Level - Light Weaver](#18th-level---light-weaver)

## 1st Level - Trick of the Light

Starting at 1st level, you have darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision, it increases its range by 60 feet.

You learn the Light and Sacred Flame cantrips. At 3rd-level you learn the Darkness and  Faerie Fire spells, which are sorcerer spells and do not count against your spells known. They can cast normally, or by expending 2 sorcery points.

## 1st Level - Flickering Aura

Starting at 1st level, you can magnify the properties of light around you. At the end of a  short or long rest, you can choose whether your magic is light-shifted or dark-shifted.

While light-shifted you can use an action to emit an aura in a 15-foot radius around you.  This area is bright light. While the aura persist, you can use a bonus action to expand or reduce its radius by 5 feet, to a maximum of 30 feet each or a minimum of 10 feet. While dark-shifted you gain the same ability, but you project an aura of nonmagical darkness. This effect lasts for 1 minute, or until you are incapacitated.

Once used, you cannot use this ability again until you finish a short or long rest

## 6th Level - Refraction Shield

At 6th level, you can manipulate the light around you for protection. As a bonus action, you can spend 2 sorcery points to magically create a shield of light or dark. The shield lasts for 1 minute, or until you are incapacitated.

While this shield is in place, whenever a creature within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack, this shield flashes with searing energy, and the attacker takes 2d6 radiant damage if you are light-shifted or 2d6 necrotic damage if you are dark-shifted.

## 14th Level - Aura Magnification

While your Flickering Aura is active, you can now expand it up to 10 feet as a bonus action, up to a maximum of 60 feet. While within the aura, a number of creatures equal to your Charisma modifier also gain the benefit of your Refraction Shield, but no more than once per turn.

Additionally, you and those creatures have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) skill checks made while light-shifted, or Dexterity (Stealth) checks made while dark-shifted. You can add your proficiency bonus when making these skill checks by expending a sorcery point.

## 18th Level - Light Weaver

Embodying both aspects of Light and Dark, you gain resistance to both radiant and necrotic damage. Additionally, as part of the action to activate your Flickering Aura, you can transform yourself and gain the following benefits for up to 1 minute:

Upon activation or as part of the bonus action to expand or reduce it's radius, you can switch to either light-shifted or dark-shifted. Each creature of your choice within your aura must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 2d8 radiant and 2d8 necrotic damage and becoming blinded till the end of their next turn on a failure, or half as much damage and not being blinded in a success.

While your Radiant Shield is active, the radius of its effect increases to 30 feet, and you deal both the radiant and the necrotic damage when hit with an attack.

Once you use this action, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you spend 5 sorcery points to use it again.

items.name: Long Bo Staff
items.description: Proficiency with a Long Bo-Staff allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

items.name: Lucerne Hammer
items.description: A pole hammer designed for armored combat, or cracking the thick hides of monsters. Counts as both a hammer and a polearm.

items.name: Luminite Armor
items.description:
Armor infused with the essence of stars. A suit of luminite armor holds with it the power to boost it's wearers abilities across a spectrum of focuses. When luminite armor is made, it can come in one of the following forms, which determines it's properties:

*   Solar - While wearing this magical armor, you have a +3 bonus to your armor class, and resistance to fire damage. Your melee weapon attacks deal an additional 1d8 fire damage, and have a +1 bonus to hit. The armor has three charges, and regains all charges daily at dawn. When a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can expend one charge as a reaction to halve the damage, and cause the attacking creature to take fire damage equal to the reduction.
*   Vortex - While wearing this magical armor, you have a +2 bonus to your armor class, and resistance to lightning damage. Your ranged weapon attacks deal an additional 1d6 lightning damage, and have a +2 bonus to hit. The armor has three charges, and regains all charges daily at dawn. As a bonus action, you can expend one charge to become invisible until you move. Attacking does not break your invisibility.
*   Nebula - While wearing this magical armor, you are considered to be proficienct with it even if you lack proficiency with heavy armor, and you are resistant to psychic damage. Your spell attacks have a +3 bonus to hit. The armor has three charges, and regains all charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can expend one charge to gain one of the following effects for one minute:
    *   Regeneration - you regain 5 hit points at the start of each of your turns.
    *   Power - each of your spells gains a 2d8 bonus to one damage roll.
    *   Mana - When you cast a spell of 3rd level or lower, roll a d6. If the result is greater than the level of the spell, you do not expend a spell slot.
*   Stardust - While wearing this magical armor, you are considered to be proficient with it even if you lack proficiency with heavy armor, and you are resistant to cold damage. Creatures you summon through class features or spells gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls. The armor has three charges, and regains all charges daily at dawn. When you summon a creature throigh a class feature or spell, you can expend one charge to summon one additional creature. This creature lasts only half as long, or for one hour in the case of indefinite durations.

items.name: Marshwarden Bow
items.description:
This bow is made of twisted cypress roots and covered in long, stringy moss. The bowstring is always slick with water.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon and nonmagical arrows fired from this bow deal an extra 1d6 acid or poison damage on a hit (your choice each time).

When you’re in a forest or swamp and are hidden from a creature, missing with an attack against it doesn’t reveal your position.

Wretched Burst. When you hit a target with an arrow fired from this bow, you can cause it to erupt in a putrid cloud centered on the target, duplicating the effect of the “Stinking Cloud” spell (save DC 15).

This version of the spell doesn’t require concentration. Once this property of the bow has been used, it can’t be used again until the next dawn or until it’s spent at least 1 hour submerged in naturally occurring swamp water.

items.name: Marvelous Magic Marker
items.description:
This quill-like tool has a narrow brush that extends from its end, which produces its own ink. You can use this marker in place of ink and a quill as calligrapher's supplies. While holding it, you can use a bonus action to mentally change the color of the ink that flows from the brush. Decorating the marker's metal casing is a dazzling spectrum of colors, which move and shimmer while the marker is being used. The brush can be retracted into the metal casing to keep it from leaving errant marks by clicking a button at the top of the marker.

While holding the marker, you can use a bonus action to triple-click its button. For the next minute, the marker magically erases any of its own-generated ink it's brushed across without a trace, instead of leaving ink behind. This effect ends early if you use a bonus action to triple-click the button again. For the duration, you can erase up to 1 square foot of the marker's ink, containing no more than 150 characters or 25 words. Once this property of the pen has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Mask of Kings
items.description:
These masks are made of dark tempered steel usually found within the infernal courts, used to mark those who bear them with a respect and leadership, and the capabilities for subterfuge that would befit a king.

While attuned to this mask, you gain the following benefits:

Your Charisma Score becomes 20, unless it is already 20 or higher.

When you see a creature, and analyze them for 1 minute or more, as an action, you may turn yourself into a perfect illusionary representation of it, mimicking both its appearance and its voice for as long as you maintain concentration. (For clarification, this illusion can be dispelled by dispel magic, amf, and can be seen through with truesight)

You have advantage on charisma checks

items.name: Mask of the Wild Beasts
items.description: These dark ceramic and gold masks are fashioned after animals that are sacred in certain cultures. Each mask's appearance and properties are based on the sacred beast it represents. While attuned to a mask, you can end your attunement to it and attune to a different one over the course of 1 minute, instead of over the course of one or more short rests.

items.name: Masquerade Mask
items.description: A blank, porcelain masquerade mask, it covers the full face and sports a pair of holes for eyes and a slit for the mouth. While it is being worn on the face, the wearer may choose to have the appearance of the mask change (no action required). It can change to any design, but must be of a style typical for a masquerade.

items.name: Maul of Unbridled Rage
items.description:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

Seething with unbridled rage and untamable anger this weapon was made to force enemies to their breaking point. This magic weapon reacts to the rage pouring out of barbarians and uses their anger to their advantage. While raging this weapon deals an additional 3d6 force damage when you hit an enemy.

items.name: Megaton Hammer
items.description:
The Megaton Hammer is a maul of extraordinary size, shape and weight. This maul is so heavy, in fact, few can lift it off the ground and fewer still can wield it in battle. The haft of this weapon is made of a sturdy wood, reinforced by adamantium, and attached to the end of this haft is a hammer head of unusual build. One side of the head is a spiked head whilst the other side is a one-foot diameter cylinder of pure metal with rivets and inscribed runes circling the side of the head. Between these two heads lies a single, fat, rounded spike that caps the end of the haft.

The Megaton Hammer has a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls and knocks back Large or smaller creatures 1d4 spaces (5-20 feet) on hit. The wielder can call upon the power held within the Megaton Hammer and strike the ground in front of them causing a fiery tremor within a 40-ft cone in front of them. Creatures caught in this cone must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw to take half of the 3d6 bludgeoning damage and 3d6 fire damage, and to avoid being knocked prone if they are a Large or smaller creature. This ability, rightfully called Earth-Shattering Strike, can not be used again until after you finish a long rest.

items.name: Millwood Giantbow
items.description:
When making an attack with this weapon, you use your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

The arrows used by this weapon costs 1000 gp (Items that create ammunition still require 1000 gp, gold is magically disappeared from your characters pouch, magic knows all).

items.name: Millwood Greatbow
items.description: When making an attack with this weapon, you use your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

items.name: Monocle of Clarity
items.description:
[![Monocle](/img/wiki/monocle.png)](/img/wiki/monocle.png)

This single lens has three filters that you can peer through. You can change the monocle's filter by rotating the lens' outer ring. A small arrow along the edge of the frame indicates the monocle's selected filter. The monocle has 4 charges and regains 1d3+1 each day at dawn. As an action, you can expend a charge to look through and activate one of the filters to gain one of several benefits:

Filter of Comprehend Languages. When you peer through this filter, you can read and comprehend any written language as per the written language portion of the comprehend languages spell.

Filter of Darkvision. While looking through this filter, you have darkvision out to a range of 60 feet.

Filter of See Invisibility. When gazing through this filter, you see invisible creatures and objects as if they were visible.

You gain the benefits of the activated filter while you wear the monocle for 1 hour or until you change the filter.

items.name: Monster Hunter Armor
items.description:
Armor of this variety is crafted from the very creatures it was designed to hunt, Dragons, Monstrosities, Undead etc.

You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.

While wearing this armor, you have advantage on attacks against all creatures this armor was made to hunt, skill checks to track this creature. Can be made of any creature type except humanoid.

items.name: Necklace of the Second Heart
items.description:
**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**:

A fine silver chain holds a small crimson gem cut to resemble a heart. The stone feels faintly warm, pulsing with a soft rhythm that never quite matches your own.

**Properties**

*   **Heart’s Reserve**. When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, the necklace flares with a pulse of red light. You instead drop to 1 hit point, and the gem dims until the next dawn.
*   **Empathic Sense.** Once per short or long rest, you can focus on the necklace to read the emotions of a creature within 30 feet that you can see. You immediately sense its strongest emotion (such as fear, anger, or calm)

items.name: Night Reaver
items.description:
## Description

 Features:

*   [3rd Level - Criticality](#3rd-level---criticality)
*   [9th Level - Rend](#9th-level---rend)
*   [13th Level - Ward of Mortality](#13th-level---ward-of-mortality)
*   [17th Level - Echo of Reaping](#17th-level---echo-of-reaping)

## 3rd Level - Criticality

Starting at 3rd level, creatures with less than half of their total hit points gain a Death Mark, granting you the following benefits:

*   You gain an additional way to use your sneak attack; you don’t need advantage on the attack roll to use your Sneak Attack against a marked creature, so long as you don’t have disadvantage on the attack roll.
*   Attack rolls against marked creatures crit on 19-20.
*   You have a bonus on attack rolls against marked creatures. This bonus equals half your proficiency bonus.

## 9th Level - Rend

Starting at 9th level, you gain the ability to **rend** those close to death.

When you hit a marked creature with your Sneak Attack, you can deal additional damage equal to your rogue level. Once you use this ability, you may not do so against until you complete a short or long rest. If you reduce a creature to zero hit points with this ability, you regain a use.

## 13th Level - Ward of Mortality

Starting at 13th level, you learn to collect and conserve the life force you steal from dying opponents, which materializes as a ward.

When you hit a marked creature with **rend**, roll your total sneak attack die. You gain temporary hit points equal to the total, unless you already have more.

## 17th Level - Echo of Reaping

Starting at 17th level, you learn to travel to and harness remnants of death.

When you hit a creature with **rend**, you may teleport to a corpse within 90 ft of you as a bonus action, and the next attack you make before the end of your next treats the target as being blood marked. In addition, through a one-minute ritual, you may travel to an area of mass death or a sight where you slew a creature within 2 miles, bringing a number of willing creatures equal to the number of creatures you have killed today (maximum of 10). Once you use this ability, you may not do so again until you complete a long rest.

items.name: Occult Seal
items.description:
This small, square talisman is inscribed with occult runes. While this seal is adhered to your spiritual focus, you gain a bonus to the saving throw DCs of your spirit arts. The bonus is determined by the seal's rarity.

When a creature targets you with an attack or forces you to make a saving throw, you can activate this seal as a reaction, allowing you to instantly tether (one tether only) to the creature. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until the next dawn.

items.name: Orb of Remembrance
items.description:
This glassy orb is 3 inches in diameter, weighs 1/2 pound, and is wrapped in a golden wire cage. Strange, wispy smoke drifts around inside the orb, creating cloud-like shapes that are almost recognizable as real-life objects.

While holding the orb, you can use an action to speak its command word, priming the orb and causing the smoke inside to restlessly billow and swirl. While holding the primed orb, you can gently squeeze it (no action required) to cause it to record that moment in time around you. When you do, the glassy sphere becomes perfectly clear over the course of 1 minute and reveals an exact duplicate of the area around you in up to a 30-foot radius as a miniature, illusory diorama inside it. While holding the orb, the scene inside soundlessly replays the next 6 seconds following the diorama's capture.

An orb remains primed for 1 minute or until used to capture a diorama. Touching a primed orb of remembrance to one that already has a diorama creates a duplicate of the diorama in the primed orb. Speaking the command word again while shaking the orb causes the diorama inside to disappear into smoke, allowing it to be primed once more.

items.name: Phial of Petulant Peculiarities
items.description: For a period of 2d4 hours after you drink this potion, any mundane inanimate object you name will materialize in your hand for 1-minute as a sentient, uncooperative version of itself. This materialized sentient object will be capable of speaking any languages you are proficient in. This potion takes the form of an indigo-colored, effervescent liquid that pours like water.

items.name: Powderkeg
items.description:
## Description

Volatile and dangerous, Powderkegs typically make use of exceedingly dangerous chemicals or magic to act as the ultimate deterrent. Despite this, Bulwarks under this specialization tend to show great care for the power they wield.


*   [3rd Level - Powderkeg Training](#3rd-level---powderkeg-training)
*   [3rd Level - Volatile Ward](#3rd-level---volatile-ward)
*   [7th Level - Combustion](#7th-level---combustion)
*   [13th level - Explosive Ward](#13th-level---explosive-ward)
*   [18th Level - Fission](#18th-level---fission)

## 3rd Level - Powderkeg Training

When you select this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with firearms and alchemist supplies.

## 3rd Level - Volatile Ward

Additionally at 3rd level, you have the ability to work volatile compounds into your armor and defensive strategies. Whenever you use your fortitude feature, the triggering creature takes fire damage equal to your bulwark level.

## 7th Level - Combustion

Starting at 7th level, your knack for mixing potent reactions extends to aid you in combat. While you are holding alchemist supplies and take the attack action, you can replace one attack on each of your turns with a burst of chemical energy.

Target a creature within 15 feet of yourself. The target must succeed a Dexterity Saving Throw (DC 8 + your Strength Modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or take 2d8 damage. This damage is acid, fire or poison (your choice).

## 13th level - Explosive Ward

When you reach 13th level, you learn to improve your Volatile Ward with even more explosive potential. When you use your Volatile Ward feature, you can choose to have each creature within 20 feet of you take the damage, instead of just the triggering creature. If you do so, the feature deals only half damage.

## 18th Level - Fission

Starting at 18th level, the explosive potential within you reaches its pinnacle. As an action, you can trigger a chain reaction within your armor to detonate out in a violent burst. You immediately take 20d6 fire damage and 20d6 bludgeoning damage, and each creature within 40 feet of you must succeed a dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + your Strength Modifier + you Proficiency Bonus) or take the same damage, taking half as much on a successful save. Objects that are not being worn or carried in the area also take this damage. After using this feature, you have immunity to all damage until the end of your next turn.

Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

items.name: Precision Gloves
items.description:
These finely crafted gloves improves the precision of  a rogue's skills. While wearing it, you gain a bonus to the saving throw DCs of your rogue features. The bonus is determined by the glove's rarity.

In addition the gloves have 3 charges that recharge at dawn. Whenever you use a cunning or devious strike you can use one of the charges instead of using  sneak die.

Uncommon: +1 DC Bonus

Rare: +2 DC Bonus

Very Rare: +3 DC Bonus

items.name: Pyromancy
items.description:
## Heart of Fire

At 1st level, whenever you start casting a spell that deals fire damage, fiery magic erupts from you. This eruption causes creatures of your choice that you can see within 10 feet of you to take fire damage equal to half your sorcerer level (minimum of 1).

## Fire in the Veins

At 6th level, you gain resistance to fire damage. In addition, spells you cast and effects you create ignore resistance to fire damage.

## Pyromancer's Fury

Starting at 14th level, when you are hit by a melee attack, you can use your reaction to deal fire damage to the attacker. The damage equals your sorcerer level, and ignores resistance to fire damage.

## Fiery Soul

At 18th level, you gain immunity to fire damage. In addition, you ignore resistance to fire damage and treat immunity to fire damage as resistance to fire damage. When treating immunity as resistance, features or feats that allow you to ignore resistance do not do so.

items.name: Quicksilver Clay
items.description:
This small orb of silver, watery liquid is strangely dry to the touch. While holding the 1-inch-diameter metallic sphere in the palm of your hand, you can mentally control the form that it takes using an action. The form stretches and turns into the imagined shape and remains centered in your hand. Regardless of the form, it can be no larger than the palm of your hand and is always a liquid. The form returns to its natural orb state when you end your focus on the quicksilver clay (no action required) or it leaves your hand.

Eating or drinking the liquid is harmless, but takes 1d4 days to exit the body.

items.name: Raven Knight
items.description:
## Knowledge of Death

When you choose this Archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the Investigation and Medicine skills. Additionally, you can make Intelligence (Investigation) and Wisdom (Medicine) checks with advantage when attempting to determine the cause of death of a creature.

## Soul Severing

At 3rd level, when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points you sever the connection of the creatures soul to its body. That creature cannot be turned into an undead. Additionally, as a reaction, when a creature is reduced to 0 hit points within 15ft of you you can empower your next attack with that creatures soul energy. Your next weapon attack deals additional necrotic damage equal to half your fighter level.

## Feather Step

At 7th level, you move with the grace of the raven. Your walking speed increases by 10ft. Additionally, as a bonus action you gain a flight speed equal to your walking speed until the end of your turn, if you end your turn in midair you drop to the ground.

## Queens Blessing

At 10th level, the Raven Queen grants you a blessing. You gain advantage on death saving throws, immunity to the frightened condition, and resistance to necrotic damage.

## Deaths Embrace

At 15th level, when you or an ally critically hit a creature, you can use your reaction to add 3d10 necrotic damage to the attacks damage roll.

## Duty in Death

At 18th level, when you are reduced to 0 hit points or killed outright you can use your reaction to make a weapon attack against the creature that killed you. On a hit you critically hit that creature and regain hit points equal to the damage dealt. You can do this once per long rest.

items.name: Raven's Claw Crossbow
items.description:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

With a bonus action you can deploy or retract the stand system.

When the stand is deployed you gain advantage on your attack rolls made by this weapon. But as a result you can't make any movement, or you lose the bonus.

items.name: Reaper’s Kusarigama
items.description:
Based on the Scythe of Reaping, this Kusarigama is adorned by grotesque engravings of tragedy, and has an obsidian lining running the length of the kusari blade, along with depictions of mass graves on the kama. Within crafting, death of any living creature must take place, each being slain with one section of the weapon before the magic may be imbued within it.

Once per turn when you hit a creature with this Kusarigama, it gains a stack of reap, which lasts until its next short or long rest. Once per turn, this weapon deals an additional 1d6 necrotic damage for every stack of reap the target has. A target may have a maximum of 5 stacks of reap. A target looses all stacks of reap if it is targeted by greater restoration, heal, or a similar restorative magic.

When a target with at least 1 stack of reap is reduced to zero hit points with this weapon, you gain 5 temporary hit points for each stack of reap the target had.

items.name: Reassemble
items.description:
: Skeleton

*   Your Constitution Score increases by 1.
*   The necromancy that stitched you together is shockingly good at keeping you intact. When you become stabilized as a result of succeeding three death saving throws, you regain hit points equal to your level.

items.name: Red Powder
items.description:
Also referred to as thermite in other settings, red powder is a volatile mixture of iron and aluminum powders. It burns rapidly and at extremely high temperatures, though it is not an explosive.

A single flask can be emptied and contains enough powder to cover one square foot. It can then be ignited, where it burns for 1 round, shedding bright light in a 30 foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. The powder can burn or melt away a single tiny non-magical item that it remains in contact with while burning.

As an action, you can recollect or brush away red powder that is yet to be ignited. Once ignited, the powder sticks to the surface and can no longer be removed until it burns away.

A creature that touches red powder, or starts its turn touching red powder, while it is burning takes 4d8 fire damage, that ignores a target's resistance to fire damage.

items.name: Ring of Cantrip Storing
items.description:
This seemingly mundane ring can store a single cantrip. Only one of these rings can be worn at a time, if another Ring of Cantrip Storing is equipped both cease to function. The ring takes one turn to equip, and one turn to unequip. The cantrip, if used for a spell that requires a save, has a DC of 10, and if used for a attack roll receive no modifiers and is treated as a straight roll. If the spell is affected by the caster's level, such as eldritch blast, it is casted at its lowest level.

A cantrip of any class can be stored within the ring. To store a cantrip, one must be able to cast the cantrip themselves, or find someone else who knows the cantrip that can store it in the ring on their behalf through a 10 minute ritual.

items.name: Ring of Emotional Suppression
items.description: This ring a golden band, with an amethyst gem and scribings in either Celestial, Draconic, or Sylvan. Activated by a spoken or mental command, the ring allows the wearer to either suppress a specific emotion, or emotion in general on themselves. The chosen effect lasts until cancelled. This does not confer the below effect. This effects takes a free action. Additionally, the ring can also be used to fight off fear and/or infatuation. When you make a saving throw to avoid or end the charmed and/or frightened conditions on yourself, you can give yourself advantage on the roll if you are wearing the ring. You must decide to do so before rolling the d20. If the save succeeds, you can't use the ring in this way until you finish a long rest. Only the wearer can receive the effects of this ring, and effects cannot stack with duplicate Rings of Emotional Suppression or Cuddly Strixhaven Mascots.

items.name: Ring of Roses
items.description:
As an action, you can cause a single rose to grow and appear in your hand. The stem is a foot long and has small, dull thorns. The rose blossoms in a color of your choice as it finishes growing, but withers and disintegrates after 24 hours.

You can cause a single rose to grow 5 times per day, which refreshes at dawn.

items.name: Ring of Silverware
items.description: While wearing this ring, you can use a bonus action to mentally command the ring to transform into a fork, spoon, or other harmless element of a set of cook's utensils (such as a ladle or spatula). The transformed ring returns to its normal form on your finger if you mentally end the effect (no action required) or are no longer holding it.

items.name: Ring of Triggered Spells
items.description: This ring stores spells cast into it, holding them until the wearer uses them. The ring can store a single level 1 spell (Casted with a first level slot), but the casting time for this spell is automatically a reaction rather than an action or bonus action. Only one of these rings can be worn at a time, if another Ring of Triggered Spells is equipped both cease to function, and you cannot cast spells from it. This trigger must be specified when the spell is stored into the ring. For example: A Cure Wounds spell is triggered as a reaction if the wearer becomes bloodied. While wearing this ring, you can cast any spell stored in it. The spell uses the slot level, spell save DC, spell attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the original caster, but is otherwise treated as if you cast the spell. The spell cast from the ring is no longer stored in it, freeing up space.

items.name: Rocket Spear
items.description:
**Rocket spear.** It's a spear with a rocket. Pressurized combustion reimagined to strap a simple oil-powered rocket to the head of a spear to deliver a rather excessive amount of melee force.

**Special** - As an action, you can fuel the rocket spear with one flask of oil. While fueled, the rocket becomes active, and the weapon's thrown range increases to 60/60. While the rocket is active and you throw the spear, you can choose to hold onto it during its flight, landing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target. Once fueled, the rocket burns out of fuel at the end of your next turn or when you make an attack with it.

items.name: Rosso di Oakheart
items.description: This 12-ounce bottle of red grape wine is created by infusing the magicked flesh of sentient fungi into the yeast during the fermentation process. When ingested in its entirety, this potent alcohol inflicts the poisoned condition. A creature poisoned in this manner is considered drunk for a number of hours equal to 1d6 - their constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hour). A creature who is normally immune to poison still suffers this effect but can choose to resist the poison as an action, ending the condition.

items.name: Sash of the Eldr Rune
items.description:
This sash of elk hair from the frigid north is as white as the endless snowy dales. The eldr (focus) rune is sewn into it with bright red threads. The sash has the following properties while you wear it:

*   Heightened Focus. As an action, you can channel the sash's magic to increase the potency of your ki. For the next minute or until you lose concentration as if concentrating on a spell, you can increase the DC of your ki abilities by 2. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn.
*   Lightened Focus. By spending 3 ki points, you can double your movement speed for 1 minute.

items.name: Scouting Knife
items.description: This dagger is adorned with a magical leaf at it's base, and is otherwise seemingly normal. This dagger may be thrown at an object or creature, at which point it will seamlessly embed itself. While embedded, the throwing creature may use it as 5 feet of stable ground no matter the height it is embedded. As a bonus action on a subsequent turn, the throwing creature may recall it through the use of a string tied to it's hilt.

items.name: Scythe
items.description: Proficiency with a scythe allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

items.name: Scythe of Reaping
items.description: This scythe is adorned by grotesque engravings of tragedy, and has an obsidian lining running the length of the blade. Each time you hit a creature with this scythe, it gains a stack of reap, which lasts until it’s next short or long rest. This weapon deals an additional 1 slashing damage for every stack of reap the target has. A target may have a maximum of 7 stacks of reap. A target looses all stacks of reap if it is targeted by greater restoration, heal, or a similar restorative magic. When a target with at least 1 stack of reap is reduced to zero hit points with this weapon, you gain 5 temporary hit points for each stack of reap the target had.

items.name: Seat Belt
items.description: While standing on a solid surface, you begin to sit down in an open space as if there were a chair beneath you. When you would normally crouch or fall to the ground when doing this, you can mentally command the belt to summon an invisible, incorporeal chair as a bonus action instead. The chair can be of any style you choose, such as a stool, recliner, throne, or rocking chair, and can support up to 500 pounds. While it is comfortable, it is nevertheless invisible and cannot be interacted with beyond simply sitting on. The chair disappears if it is no longer on a solid surface, you stand up, or if you're pushed or moved while sitting on it.

items.name: Sentinel Blade
items.description:
The Sentinel Blade embodies the essence of unwavering defense, blending masterful swordplay with unbreakable resolve. Trained to stand as the ultimate shield for their allies, these warriors adopt a specialized stance that turns every strike against them into an opportunity. Their blades move with precision, deflecting blows with ease and delivering calculated ripostes.

## *Aegis Flow*

Starting at 2nd level, you learn to develop flow during the midst of combat, building on each success as you begin to flow like a dance. While using a melee weapon without a shield, whenever you land a successful hit your first time in a turn, an attack misses you, or you succeed on a saving throw from a hostile creature, you gain flow equal to 1d6 + half your total mastery. Whenever you take damage from an attack, you lose flow instead. If this damage reduces your flow to 0, you take any remaining damage. If you reduce an attack's damage to zero, you suffer no additional effects from the weapon. You can have a max amount of flow equal to twice your practitioner level, which depletes at the end of combat. You can only build flow whilst in combat.

## Riposte

Starting at 6th level, when an attack misses you, or you block one with flow completely, you may make a melee attack as a reaction against the target. Additionally, you may instead spend 1 mastery point to use a technique.

## Vigilant Guardian

Starting at 14th level, when an ally within melee range is hit by an attack, you can use your flow to block the attack as if it were targeting you.

## Blade Dance

Starting at 18th level, you learn to harness your flow into focus. When you have at least 75% of your maximum flow and focus on a technique or use a technique through riposte, you may sacrifice 7 flow to spend 1 less mastery point.

The following goes with the subclass as part of the practitioner class

## Unyielding Sentinel | Mastery

### Form 1: Flowing Strike

: Physical

**Prerequisites**: Novice, Sentinel Blade

Make a weapon attack against a creature or object. The attack deals a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus in additional damage.

**Focused (1 point)**

Hit or miss, roll a number or d4s equal to half your total mastery. You gain flow equal to the total.

### Form 2: Windfall Step

**Type**: Physical

**Prerequisites**: Intermediate, Sentinel Blade

You move in a mirage, teleporting to an area within 60ft of you.

**Focused (1 point, 0 if you have your maximum flow).**

You can use another technique that includes a weapon attack. This may be before or after you teleport.

### Form 3: Ebon Tide

**Type**: Mental

**Prerequisites**: Advanced, Sentinel Blade

You may temporarily steel yourself against other manners of attacks. Until the end of your next turn, you may block damage from any source using Aegis Flow.

**Focused (2 points, 1 if you your maximum flow).**

The duration of this technique becomes 1 minute. Until the end of your next turn, if you would succeed on a saving throw and normally take half damage, you would instead take no damage.

### Form 4: Dance of Death

**Type**: Physical

**Prerequisites**: Master, Sentinel Blade

**Duration**: 1 minute

For the next minute, your maximum flow and the flow you gain is doubled. During this time, you have vulnerability to attacks while your flow is 0.

You must use an action to end this effect

**Focused (1 points).**

You may use another technique from this form

items.name: Shock Cube
items.description: This cube deals 1 point of lightning damage to any creature that touches it, or starts its turn touching it. once it has dealt 20 lightning damage this way it loses this property for 1 hour.

items.name: Shock Hypercube
items.description:
This 3-inch cube crackles with electric potential energy, and resonates slightly when touched. Any creature that ends its turn touching the cube takes lightning damage equal to half their remaining hitpoints. Additionally, the creature cannot take reactions until the end of their next turn.

Once the cube has discharged, it cannot do so again until 1 hour has passed.

items.name: Silver Bow
items.description:
Made of a mysterious yet flexible space-metal alloy, this bow gleams a radiant silver-white, like moonlight. The Silver Bow grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it, and deals radiant damage instead of piercing damage.

While attuned to the Silver Bow, you gain the following properties:

items.name: Siren’s Crescent
items.description: This finely crafted scimitar features a crescent-shaped blade adorned with intricate engravings. The blade is perforated with small holes and wind chambers, allowing it to emit a haunting, flute-like melody when swung or spun.

items.name: Slug Shotgun
items.description:
Using top-of-the-line polymorphic magic, a slug shotgun converts standard shells and ammunition into live slugs!

The weapon loses both the \[Point-Blank\] and \[Greater Point-Blank\] properties if it has them, and deals 1d4 extra acid damage, or 2d4 extra acid damage if the weapon had the \[Greater Point-Blank\] property.

items.name: Snowball Mittens
items.description: These cozy, knitted mittens have been enchanted to always bring a touch of winter with them. As an action while wearing the mittens, you can clamp your hands together, as if you were forming a snowball. When you open your hands again, a perfectly formed snowball appears in them. Any snow created in this way vanishes after 1 minute.

items.name: Solar Six-Shooter
items.description:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Additionally, this weapon deals radiant damage instead of piercing damage.

This weapon has 6 charges. Once per turn, you can expend one charge when you attack with this weapon. If this attack hits, it deals 6d10 extra radiant damage, and the target must succeed on a DC18 constitution saving throw or be blinded until the end of it's next turn.

The weapon regains a single charge when it spends a full 8 hours in direct sunlight.

items.name: Spellbreaker
items.description:
## Description

Bulwarks who spend their training bettering themselves against the ever-present threat of magic. Spellbreakers are the last thing a wizard wants to see on the field of battle, usually resulting in a fairly one sided encounter.


*   [3rd Level - Out of the Inferno](#3rd-level---out-of-the-inferno)
*   [7th Level - Spell-Shatter](#7th-level---spell-shatter)
*   [13th Level - Anticipation](#13th-level---anticipation)
*   [18th Level - Willbreaker](#18th-level---willbreaker)

## 3rd Level - Out of the Inferno

When you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you learn to incorporate methods to help you avoid magical effects into your stance. While your defensive stance is active, you also gain resistance to fire, cold, lightning, and acid damage.

## 7th Level - Spell-Shatter

Starting at 7th level, magical warding effects become inefficient at halting your progress. When you hit a creature or object with a weapon attack, all spells from the abjuration school immediately end if they are affecting the target.

## 13th Level - Anticipation

When you reach 13th level, you gain an understanding and fortitude against the ways that mages tend to fight. When you enter your defensive stance, you automatically succeed on the next intelligence, wisdom, or charisma saving throw you make before it ends.

## 18th Level - Willbreaker

At 18th level, your magical defenses allow you to nearly completely ignore the effects of lesser magic, making your foes need to drain their resources to even hope to defeat you. You are immune to the effects of cantrips and 1st and 2nd level spells, unless you wish to be affected by them.

items.name: Spiked Cestus
items.description: Proficiency with a cestus allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

items.name: Spy Lord’s Scepter
items.description:
A sleek scepter of darkened mithril and carved obsidian gemstone, fitted with an elongated hilt and stylized silver in the shape of winding serpents and veiled masks. The shaft tapers into a ritual blade sheathed flush into the bottom of the rod. To all appearances, it is a ceremonial staff unless drawn and wielded with purpose.

While attuned to this item, you can use an action to cast the Message or Detect Thoughts spells (save DC 20). You may also use it as a spellcasting focus gaining the following benefits:

items.name: Staff of Elemental Harmony
items.description:
This finely crafted quarterstaff is made of a smooth, polished wood and bears intricate carvings depicting swirling elemental patterns along its length. This quarterstaff has 10 elemental charges that recharges at dawn, and it grants its wielder the following abilities while they are attuned to the staff:

*

items.name: Star Blade
items.description:
## Description

You have spent many a night training under the stars and became touched by cosmic energy to shape light as you see fit. Creating armaments of pure light and radiance you become a beacon for those lost in the dark.


*   [3rd Level - Hardlight](#3rd-level---hardlight)
*   [One With the Stars](#7th-level---one-with-the-stars)
*   [Astral Multitool](#7th-level---astral-multitool)
*   [Shooting Stars](#10th-level---shooting-stars)
*   [Galactic Aegis](#15th-level---galactic-aegis)
*   [Stellar Expanse](#18th-level---stellar-expanse)

## 3rd Level - Hardlight

3rd-Level Feature

At 3rd level, you gain an amount of Hardlight charges equal to your double proficiency bonus, resetting on a long rest. You can use one charge to conjure a mundane melee weapon you are proficient with out of radiant light. This weapon does radiant damage, and counts as a magical weapon. Alternatively, you can spend one charge to conjure up an instrument or tool you have proficiency with. Equipment conjured up using this feature lasts an hour.

At 15th level this changes from 1 hours to 8 hours.

## 7th Level - One With the Stars

7th-Level Feature

At 7th level, you gain a bonus to damage and attack rolls and ability checks with Hardlight equipment equal to half your Wisdom modifier.

## 7th Level - Astral Multitool

7th-Level Feature

At 7th level, you can spend a bonus action to change your Hardlight equipment into another piece of equipment. You can use this feature once per short rest.

## 10th Level - Shooting Stars

10th-Level Feature

Starting at 10th level, your mastery over hardlight weapons has granted you the ability to create ranged weapons. Creating a ranged weapons costs 2 hardlight and a bonus action to create. The ammunition it uses looks like a shooting star as you fire the weapons, and does not consume a hardlight charge.

## 15th Level - Galactic Aegis

15th-Level Feature

At 15th level, you can spend one Hardlight charge to create Hardlight armor. Hardlight armor counts as magical heavy armor, and has a base AC of 10 + your Dexterity modifier+ your Wisdom modifier. If a creature other than yourself wears this armor, it loses its properties.

Additionally, you can spend one Hardlight charge to create a Hardlight shield. This shield counts as a magical shield that provides 3/4 cover for yourself, this shield does not provide the base AC of a shield. If a creature other than yourself equips this shield, it loses its properties.

## 18th Level - Stellar Expanse

18th-Level Feature

At 18th level, you can spend 2 Hardlight charges to empower yourself for one minute. Whenever you hit a creature with a Hardlight weapon, the weapon does an additional 3d6 radiant damage. While you have a Hardlight shield equipped, if an enemy misses you with a weapon attack, you can use your reaction to gain advantage on all attack rolls against them until the end of your next turn.

Additionally, you can maintain one piece of Hardlight equipment indefinitely. While this equipment exists, your total amount of Hardlight charges is reduced by one until it is dismissed.

items.name: Star Gels
items.description: With the adhesive property of your typical ooze and the florescent spores of certain mushrooms, these special gels can be attached to the walls or ceilings of rooms. Under dim light or darkness, they glow a light-tinted green providing dim light at a 15 ft radius. The gels, mimicking the stars, can also inspire a viewer and calm their nerves via the release of sweet-scented spores. If a creature spends a short rest in a room full of gels (gazing upon them), they gain 1d8 temp hp that wears off by their next LR. Once this property is used, it cannot be used again by any creature till the next evening, when the real stars first emerge.

items.name: Storm Chaser
items.description:
## Storm Chaser Magic

Starting at 3rd level, you learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Storm Chaser Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn’t count against the number of ranger spells you know.



| Ranger Level | Spells |
| --- | --- |
| 3rd | Thunderwave |
| 5th | Vortex Warp |
| 9th | Call Lightning |
| 13th | Ice Storm |
| 17th | Control Winds |



## Firearm Proficiency

At 3rd Level, you have learned to harness the potency of firearms to complement the powers of the storms you chase. You gain proficiency with firearms.

## Thundercrack

Starting at 3rd level, when you take this archetype at 3rd level, you are able to unleash a deafening boom when you use your ranged weapons. Once on each of your turns, when you attack with a ranged weapon, you can force each creature within 10 feet of you to make a constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes 1d8 thunder damage. The damage increases to 2d8 at 11th level.

## Charged Shell

At 7th level, when you take the attack action, you can forgo your extra attack feature to make a single ranged weapon attack. This attack deals triple the damage dice of the weapon you used, and deals lightning damage instead of bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing. This attack is also critical on a d20 roll of 19 or 20 if used during a storm.

## Storm Caller

At 11th level, you have learned to bring storms to you. As an action, you call forth a vortex at a point you can see within 60 feet. The vortex is a cylinder with a radius of 30 feet, and a height of 90 feet. The vortex counts as a storm for the purposes of Charged Shell.

As a bonus action on each of your turns while the vortex lasts, you can target a creature inside, striking them with a bolt of lightning. The target must make a dexterity saving throw, taking 3d10 lightning on a failure, or half as much on a successful save.

The vortex lasts for 10 minutes, and once used, cannot be summoned again until you finish a long rest, or spend a spell slot of at least 3rd level or higher to summon it again. No more than 1 vortex can exist at once.

## Eye of the Storm

At 15th level, you gain resistance to lightning and thunder damage.

Additionally, you can target yourself with the bolt of lightning from your storm caller ability. If you do so, instead of taking damage, your next charged shell attack deals extra damage equal to twice the amount of damage the lightning would have dealt. This also causes the attack to be critical on a d20 roll of 18, 19, or 20.

items.name: Sub-Ninth Rifle
items.description:
This large sniper rifle is made with thicker material to propel projectiles at very long distances without losing accuracy, delivering destructive capabilities that can even pierce metal gates. Each shot made with this rifle sends a loud ripple on the battlefield that would bring dread whether or not they will be the next target. The obvious drawback to this rifle is its size and weight, making it difficult to move while in combat, as well as its noise, making it not really ideal for assassinations unless you are far enough, thus only a few choice marksmen challenge themselves in wielding this weapon. The creator explained that the reason for its name is because he " . . . wanted to make a weapon that can match the power of 9th-level spells" but it has yet to be seen in the results.

On a hit, this weapon deals 14d12 instead of 2d12. While equipped or simply on your body, your movement speed is reduced to a maximum of 10 feet. Each attack made with this weapon produces an explosive boom that is audible within 600 feet (the creator didn't factor in a silencer when making this weapon, thus trying to apply one would be ill-advised as it would just blow up at the first shot). This rifle deals maximum damage when you hit structures or objects that are not worn.

Special - An enhanced spyglass is mounted to the weapon. While using it, creatures and objects are magnified to four times their size.

This precision rifle can't be reloaded usign a bonus action

items.name: Sundisk
items.description:
The Sundisk is a magical throwning weapon used to destroy hordes of enemies. The Sundisk has 5 charges. As an action, you can expend a charge to throw it in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. All creatures in that area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw with a DC of 15 + your dexterity modifier, taking 3d6 radiant damage on a failed save.

Once the disk reaches its maximum range of 30 feet, it remains in that space. You can use a bonus action to make the Sundisk return to you along the shortest path possible. All creatures in the area of the return path must repeat the same saving throw, taking 6d6 radiant damage on a failed save.

The Sundisk returns to you on your next turn even if you had moved since throwing the disk. All expended charges are regained at dawn when the sun's rays touch the disk.

Once the Sundisk have expended all charges, it can act as a magic boomerang.

items.name: Sunlight Globe
items.description:
This small sphere of thick glass weighs 1 pound. If you are within 60 feet of it, you can speak one of two command words and cause it to emanate 60ft radius of daylight.

When activated, it will start dim and grow to match the time of day over the first hour.

Once used, the daylight effect can't be used again until the next dawn.The light follows a set pattern, granting 12 hours of daylight before dimming to dim light or no light (determined by the command word spoken) for 12 hours, then repeating.

You can speak another command word as an action to make the illuminated globe rise into the air and float no more than 5 feet off the ground. The globe hovers in this way until you or another creature grasps it.

items.name: Survivalist's Wayfinder
items.description:
This handheld device has four crystaline buttons that are each associated with a different element. The device has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the device, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges and press a button. Each button causes the device to swivel and point like a compass to the nearest relevant location, as determined by the button, for 1 hour or until another button is pressed. If there's nothing for the device to point to, it spins in place for the duration or until it's within range of something to point to.

Air. If you're inside or underground, the device points to the nearest outdoor exit within 1 mile of you. If you're outside, it points in the direction of the nearest storm within 50 miles of you.

Earth. The device points to the nearest cave entrance that's Medium or larger and within 1 mile of you. If you're underground, it points in the direction of the nearest unpolished gemstone within 100 feet of you.

Fire. The device points to the nearest active campfire, firepit, forge, or hearth that's within 1 mile of you.

Water. The device points to the nearest source of fresh, drinkable water that's within 1 mile of you.

items.name: Sword Saint
items.description:
## Sword Saint


### Weapon Pledge

At 2nd level, choose a weapon you are proficient in to pledge your devotion to (ex. longsword, shortbow, glaive, etc). When wielding the a weapon, the following features are made available to you.

#### Flying Weapon

You learn how to wield the weapon without needing to even touch it. You can use a bonus action to move the weapon up to 30 feet in any direction or teleport it back to your hand instantly. You can attack and use techniques with the weapon as if you were standing in the weapon’s space.

While the weapon is being controlled in this way, it is treated as if you were still holding it (you require a free hand to attack with it, or two hands if it’s two-handed, etc). You cannot move it more than 120 feet away from yourself, and if brought outside of this range, you cannot control it further until it’s within the range.

#### Draining Strike

If you kill a hostile creature with an attack made with your flying weapon, you can use a reaction to drain the creature, regaining mastery points equal to half your maximum (rounded up). After you use this feature, you must take a short or long rest before you can use it again.

### Weapon Devotee

At 6th level, you become increasingly adept at using your weapon for techniques. In the same bonus action that you use to move the weapon, you can spend 1 mastery point to use the primary effect of a technique you know. Beginning at 14th level, you can focus the technique you use.

### Weapon Mount
