At 14th level, your control over the weapon you devoted yourself to extends even to being able to ride it. Your weapon can be ridden in the air, during which it cannot be used for techniques. It has a flying speed equal to your movement speed plus an additional 30 feet and can carry up to 500 pounds before it can no longer fly.

### Armamentarium

At 18th level, you can control two flying weapons at once, with the second not being treated as if you were holding it. If you use a bonus action to use a technique with one, the other can make a single weapon attack.

items.name: Teleportation Codex
items.description: In this tome is inscribed 2 teleportation circles that come with it(DM discretion on which ones). Throughout your travels as you study and find new Teleportation Circles you can take 1 minute to write them in this tome in order to be able to reference them later. This book has 3 charges regaining all expended charges each day at dawn. You can use these charges to either use Teleport(1charge) or Teleportation Circle(2 charges) using any circles you have inscribed inside the codex. Flip the book to the page with the Teleportation Circle you wish to go and speak the command word.

items.name: Temperature Controlled Lining
items.description:
This jar of mysterious purplish viscous goo was discovered to have exceptional insulative properties and can resist temperature changes for an extremely long time, it is often imbued with a little bit of magic to allow the user to alter the temperature of it like a cooling/heating unit

Over the course of 10 minutes, a creature can apply this goo to any armor or clothing, the goo doesn't impact movement or agility at all, and after application, any creature wearing the armor or clothing ignores the effect of extreme heat and extreme cold

items.name: The Extraplanar
items.description:
## Extended Spell List

The Extraplanar lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

Spell Level - Spells:



| Sorcerer Level | Spells |
| --- | --- |
| 1st | Bane, Sanctuary |
| 2nd | Arcanist's Magic Aura, Vortex Warp |
| 3rd | Bestow Curse, Glyph of Warding |
| 4th | Giant Insect, Summon Elemental |
| 5th | Creation, Summon Celestial |



## Planar Tether

When you form this pact at 1st level, you are granted the ability to tether your summoned creatures to your plane, granting them additional strengths.

When you cast a warlock spell that summons a single creature, you may undergo a special ritual during the casting of the spell. Doing so changes the spell's casting time to 10 minutes, and the summon lasts until it is dispelled. If the spell normally requires concentration, you may ignore the requirement.

When you summon a creature in this way, it is referred to as your tether, and it gains the following benefits:

*   You can replace its hit points with 5 x your warlock level.
*   It adds your proficiency bonus to its damage rolls.
*   If the creatures to-hit bonus is less than that of your spell attack bonus, it may instead use your spell attack bonus when attacking.

You learn the Find Familiar spell, it counts as a warlock spell for you, and it does not count towards your total spells known.

If you summon another creature using this ritual, any previous creatures summoned by it are immediately dispelled. After you complete this ritual, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

## Ethereal Stores

Starting at 6th level, you can briefly store items away for later use. You can cast the \[Wristpocket\] spell a number of times equal to your charisma modifier without expending a spell slot. When you do, the stored object is sent to the ethereal plane, and the spell does not require concentration. You regain all expended uses of this ability when you finish a long rest.

Additionally, you can see into the ethereal plane up to 10 ft.

## Undying Binds

At 10th level, when tether drops to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to expend a pact slot. Doing so will restore 50 hit points to the summon.

## Sacrificial Summoning

Starting on 14th level, while your Planar Tether summon is active, you can use an action to sacrifice part of yourself to empower it and your spells, doing so allows you to channel this ability until your next long rest, or until you end it as an action. While channeling this ability, you have the following effects:

*   Your hit point maximum is reduced by 30.
*   You have disadvantage on Constitution checks and saving throws, except for those made to maintain concentration.
*   Your tether has advantage on all saving throws
*   You can use a bonus action to give your tether temporary hit points equal to your warlock level
*   Creatures within 10 ft of your tether have disadvantage on saving throws against your spells.

If you end this ability early, you cannot use it again until you finish a long rest.

items.name: Thermal Hypercube
items.description: This 3-inch cube of condensed brimstone resonates slightly when touched, and generates enough dry heat to keep the temperature within 30 feet of it at 120 degrees Fahrenheit (~49 degrees Celsius). This area is subject to extreme heat.

items.name: Thornmail
items.description:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.

When an attacker hits you with a melee attack, they take 1d8 magical piercing damage and can't regain hit points until the end of its next turn.

Additionally, when you are grappled or restrained by a creature by making direct contact with you, they can't regain hit points until they end the condition and any damage dealt from feats or class features that does damage via grappling or being grappled counts as magical when wearing this armor.

items.name: Thought Gem
items.description:
A Thought Gem is a smooth, hemispherical precious stone that swirls with the memories of past bearers While holding the gem, you can use the gem to store created thought strands (as with the Encode Thoughts spell) indefinitely, as well as recall them at a later time. It also functions as a spellbook for you, where you can retrieve and store information and spells within the gem at the same rate as reading and writing, its spells and other writing psychically encoded within it. While holding it, you become aware of the spells and thought strands stored within it, and can use it as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

You can cast encode thoughts as an action, with the duration lasting until dispelled, and the thought strand disappearing into the gem. Casting this spell while focusing on a particular thought strand allows you to instantly receive whatever memory, idea, or message the thought strand contains. (Casting detect thoughts on the Thought Gem has the same effect, but the thought strand is determined at random unless using the deeper probe option of the spell.)

items.name: Thumper
items.description:
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit with a melee attack using this weapon, it deals an extra 1d6 damage. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to bludgeoning damage.

Attack rolls made with this weapon can't be hindered with spells and magical effects that increase the target's Armor Class except for the Haste spell(i.e. A target won't benefit from the bonus to AC from a Shield spell or Mage Armor spell against this magic weapon's attack rolls) as well as ignoring the magic bonuses of magical items.

When your attack roll is a critical hit, the target must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Thunderlord's Maul
items.description: This weapon was once wielded by the Thunderlord's an ancient paladin order that delivered swift and thunderous justice to those who opposed order.  Only the strongest of the Thunderlord's could wield this large hammer. It glows a deep electric blue, has runes that say "Thunderous Wrath". The head of the maul is shaped like a large thundercloud and appears to have a deep blue flashes across the head as if it trapped a thunderbolt waiting to be released.

items.name: Thunderstrike Musket
items.description:
This magical musket is imbued with the essence of thunder and has the following properties when attuned:

*

items.name: Tidepiercer
items.description: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this magic weapon. Tidepiercer is a spellcasting focus for you.

items.name: Totem of Inception
items.description: What appears to be a typical paperweight has an innate magical quality to it, which is invoked when the paperweight is first touched by someone. When spun by the linked owner of the paperweight, it always loses momentum after exactly 6 seconds. If anyone other than the linked owner spins it the totem continues spinning until an outside force inhibits it.

items.name: Tower Shield
items.description:
*50 gp, 20lbs - AC +0*

Also known as a Scutum, the tower shield is a large piece of curved metal or wood, or a composite of both. It is the size of it's user, too big and it is unwieldy if not impossible to use, and too small it becomes ineffective.

The shield confers no AC bonus to it's user by default. Instead it grants a damage threshold to you and creatures that you are providing cover for, so long as they are the same size or smaller than the shield. The damage threshold equals your strength modifier, plus your proficiency bonus when wielded with one hand, or twice your strength modifier plus your proficiency bonus when wielded with two. The damage threshold increases for each allied tower shield wielder within 5 feet, adding up to two of their proficiency bonuses to the damage threshold.

A creature with a strength score less than 13 has their speed lowered by 10 feet while wielding a tower shield.

This shield cannot be used as a base for an animated shield.

items.name: Tracking Bookmark
items.description: This small and lovingly hand-made bookmark comes in all shapes and sizes, but is perfect for any bookworms that need to keep track of their spot while perusing through thick tomes. Upon speaking a command word and flipping open the book, the tome the bookmark carries will magically adjust and flip directly to the marked page. Each tome can only contain one bookmark at a time.

items.name: Treblecuff
items.description: While wearing this ear cuff, you can add half your proficiency bonus to any ability check you make to play an instrument if you aren't proficient with it. In addition, you can magically discern (and consequently recreate) the note of any sound you hear.

items.name: Trick Card
items.description: Ostensibly an ordinary playing card, though imbued with a subtle illusion magic. One attuned with it can, with intent, tap the back of the card to change its number or suit. A single-fingered tap iterates the number by one, a tap with two fingers alternates between the four suits, and a tap with three fingers changes the design of the card to match any other card it touches.

items.name: Trinket of Freshness
items.description:
This item generates an aura of magical freshness by keeping the air in a 10ft by 10ft cube centered on the trinket clean, you can use an action to change what the aura smells like.

Food and other perishables do not decay while in the aura generated by the trinket.

items.name: True Path
items.description:
## True Path

The True Path requires the constant repetition of martial forms until absolute perfection is achieved. With perfection comes “truth”, an aspect of life that such practitioners would consider their peak.

### Truest Strike

At 2nd level, you learn the True Strike cantrip and can cast it as a bonus action. When you cast it, you gain its benefits on the current turn rather than the next turn, and it doesn’t require concentration for you. The spell ends at the end of your turn.

Once per short or long rest, if you kill a hostile creature affected by True Strike, you can use your reaction to regain mastery points equal to half your maximum (rounded up).

### Truth Beyond Doubt

At 6th level, when you strike true, you can bypass the defenses of your enemies. A technique’s attack that has advantage due to True Strike’s effect ignores an enemy’s resistances. At 18th level, such an enemy’s damage immunities are treated as resistances (which cannot be ignored).

### Verify

At 14th level, when you cast true strike on a target, you can learn their resistances, immunities, and current hit points. Additionally, you gain advantage on insight checks against the target.

### Perfect Strike

At 18th level, you can analyze the intrinsic truth about a creature. When you cast True Strike, you can spend 1 mastery point to give your next technique’s attack a +10 bonus to the attack roll and have the creature make their next save against the technique with disadvantage.

items.name: Tubes of Trickery
items.description:
This drab pair of empty ceramic cylinders are magically linked. Each vessel is 3 inches in diameter and one foot tall. Opening one of the vessel's lids and placing an item inside will magically teleport it to the second tube, making a quiet whoosh sound when it does. A very quiet thud can be heard when receiving an item. A tube cannot hold more than 2 pounds of materials at any time, and both lids must be closed for the items to be teleported. Items can only be sent if the two tubes are on the same plane of existence. A creature cannot be sent through the tubes. Only one tube must be empty, or else nothing occurs. The tubes must be within a 30 mile radius of one another.

You can only use this property once per day, which refreshes at dawn.

items.name: Tuneful Earcuffs
items.description: Taking inspiration from an Earring of Message and a pair of Sending Stones, these metallic earcuffs are enchanted with evocation magic to recreate sounds or melodies from memory. This item has charges equal to your proficiency bonus that reset on a long rest: with an action, you can expend one charge to cause song familiar to you to play at a volume that is only audible to you. The song plays until completion in which the cuffs deactivate, requiring another charge to repeat the song or play a new one. You have disadvantage on perception checks related to hearing while this item is activated.

items.name: Unfettered
items.description:
## Description:

Wearing heavy armors tends to take immense physical strain, and as such usually restricts the movements of the wearer. Bulwarks who call themselves Unfettered work tirelessly to be just as, if not more efficient with their movements while donning these heavy suits.


[3rd Level - Heightened Agility](#3rd-level---heightened-agility)

[3rd Level - Armored Movement](#3rd-level---armored-movement)

[7th Level - Iron Fist](#7th-level---iron-fist)

[13th Level - Quaking Vault](#13th-level---quaking-vault)

[18th Level - Weightless Steel](#18th-level---weightless-steel)

## 3rd Level - Heightened Agility

At 3rd level when you select this specialization, you learn to move with better flow while wearing heavy armor. It weighs half as much to you, and it does not apply disadvantage on your dexterity (stealth) checks.

## 3rd Level - Armored Movement

Additionally at 3rd level, you learn to move yourself with considerable speed for being weighed down. Your walking speed increases by 10 feet, and increases by another 10 feet at 18th level.

## 7th Level - Iron Fist

At 7th level, your movements are swift and fluid enough to carry the full force of your armor with them. When you make an unarmed attack while wearing heavy armor, you can choose to treat it as being a weapon with the heavy property, and you can roll a d8 in place of the normal damage of your unarmed attacks.

## 13th Level - Quaking Vault

Starting at 13th level, your poise and agility work in conjunction to make landing less deadly to you, and more so to those around you. You become resistant to falling damage while wearing heavy armor, and landing no longer knocks you prone. When you land while wearing heavy armor, creatures within 15 feet of you take 2d10 bludgeoning damage, and must succeed on a strength saving throw (DC 8 + your strength modifier + your proficiency bonus) or be knocked prone. A creature can only be affected by this ability once per turn.

## 18th Level - Weightless Steel

At 18th level, heavy armor ceases to be a burden for you in any meaningful way. Heavy armor weighs nothing to you while you are wearing it, and you may treat it as if it were medium armor for the purposes of adding your dexterity modifier to your armor class.

items.name: Updated Flame Tongue
items.description: You can use a bonus action to speak this magic weapon's command word, causing flames to erupt from it. These flames shed bright light in a 40-foot radius and dim light for an additional 40 feet. While the weapon is ablaze, it deals an extra 2d6 fire damage to any target it hits with a melee weapon attack. The flames last until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you drop or sheathe the weapon.

items.name: Verdant Breastplate
items.description:
When found, this breastplate resembles a leather cuirass covered in large, dried leaves. Once you wear and attune to it, however, it springs to life and flourishes; its leaves remain pliable and flexible, but harden against incoming harm to protect you. Sleeping in this armor has no adverse effect on you.

While wearing this armor, moving through nonmagical plants that impede movement (such as by creating difficult terrain) costs you no extra movement, and you don’t take damage from nonmagical plant hazards such as thorns or spines. In addition, the “entangle” spell has no effect on you, and you have advantage on any Dexterity (Stealth) check you make to hide in areas of dense foliage.

𝘾𝙝𝙖𝙧𝙜𝙚𝙨. This armor has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you’re a druid or ranger and have cast the “plant growth” spell from the armor within the last 24 hours, the armor regains all expended charges instead.

𝙎𝙥𝙚𝙡𝙡𝙨. While wearing this armor, you can use an action to expend some of its charges to cast either the “entangle” (1 charges) or “plant growth” (3 charges) spell from it, using a spell save DC of 15. If you’re a druid or ranger, you can choose to cast the spell from the armor using your spell save DC instead.

When you cast “plant growth” using this armor, the plants affected by the spell are enriched for 30 days. The plants yield twice the normal amount of food when harvested.

items.name: Versipellis Chain
items.description: While attuned to a this ornate chain, you may alter it's shape as an action. The chain may be coaxed into a bracelet, necklace, earring, or similar jewelry so long as its total length is no more than 18". As part of the same action used to change the chains shape, jewels ,charms or other trinkets may be fixed to the chain, which reshapes to form custom settings as desired. The chain may be reshaped indefinitely, so long as it does not reach a total length of more than 18" - 1" /per attached trinket.

items.name: Vial Ring
items.description:
This ring is made of magic glass (That cannot be broken in ring-form) and has a cork stopper at its top like a set gemstone. You can unstopper the ring (no action required) to magically transform it into a small glass vial. The vial holds up to 1 ounce of liquid. The mouth of the vial and ring are both a half inch wide, allowing the cork to remain the same size even when the ring is transformed. When the vial is stoppered, it magically returns to its ring form. Anything contained by the vial magically fits within the ring while it's transformed.

While the ring itself is magical, the cork is not. Any cork of the right size can be used to stopper the ring or vial, allowing you to mark the corks as needed or replace them, should they become lost or destroyed by acid or other similar hazardous (albeit bottleable) materials.

items.name: Vocal Drops
items.description: This hand-held leather pouch of multi-colored gumdrops can be made in batches of 1d10+5 candies at a time. When ingesting one of these gumdrops, your vocal qualities are enhanced. You gain advantage on performance checks involving vocals for 10 minutes. However, if you ingest more than one gumdrop at a time, you lose your voice for 10 minutes instead.

items.name: Voidknights Maul
items.description:
Voidknight’s Maul

Weapon (Maul), very rare

Attunement

Requires attunement by a Paladin

Description

The crystalline head of this magic hammer has been bathed in the latent magic of the Astral Plane. While you remain attuned to it, you age at one-tenth your normal rate.

𝘼𝙨𝙩𝙧𝙖𝙡 𝙎𝙢𝙞𝙩𝙚. Whenever you use your Divine Smite feature against a creature, you can choose for it to deal psychic damage instead of radiant damage.

Furthermore, when you expend a spell slot of 2nd level or higher to use your Divine Smite, you can force the target of the attack to make a Constitution saving throw against your paladin spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature is affected by the “levitate” spell.

This version of the spell can affect multiple creatures at once, though you must still concentrate on the spell as normal.

If your concentration on the spell ends, any affected creature gently floats back to the ground. Otherwise, you can use your action to move one or multiple targets affected by the spell vertically, up to 20 feet, dividing the movement between them.

Each affected creature must remain within the spell’s range

items.name: Vorpal Weapon
items.subtype: any melee weapon that deals slashing damage
items.description:
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to slashing damage.

When you attack a creature that has at least one head with this weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, you cut off one of the creature's heads. The creature dies if it can't survive without the lost head. A creature is immune to this effect if it is immune to slashing damage, doesn't have or need a head, has legendary actions, or the GM decides that the creature is too big for its head to be cut off with this weapon. Such a creature instead takes an extra 6d8 slashing damage from the hit.

items.name: Warmog's Armor
items.description:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor. While you don this armor, your hit point maximum increases by 30.

You regain 10 hit points at the start of your turn. If you take damage, this feature doesn't function at the start of your next turn.

items.name: Weapon of Sniping
items.subtype: any weapon with the range or thrown property
items.description: When you make an attack with this weapon, the short and long range is doubled.

items.name: Weapon, +4
items.description: You have a +4 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

items.name: Whip of Constriction
items.description:
If you roll a 20 on an attack roll against a creature you can choose to automatically grapple the creature. A creature grappled in this way is restrained. While grappling a creature with this whip the wielder can’t move further than the reach of the whip from the creature, and can't attack with the whip, without releasing the grapple(takes no action).

A creature grappled in this way can use an action to make a strength (Athletics) or dexterity (acrobatics) check with a DC 13 to break the grapple.

items.name: Wind-Up Phoenix
items.description:
This carefully constructed automaton was constructed in the image of a Phoenix. When you speak the command word, small illusory flames spring to life within its empty eye sockets and the gears begin to turn, causing the small mechanical bird to fly around the speaker in circles and occasionally perch on their shoulder. This effect end if the distance between the toy and its owner is 25 feet or greater.

Inscribed on one wing of this bird is the ornate depiction of a tiny smoldering Phoenix egg, a signature left there by the toy's designer.

items.name: Windswept Kite
items.description:
[![Windswept KITE](/img/wiki/windswept_kite.png)](/img/wiki/windswept_kite.png)



This silk kite (colors, shape, etc. are customizable) is 5 feet across and is attached to a 60-foot string. It weighs 1 pound, can carry up to one pound in weight, and can be folded into a 6-inch square. While holding the kite, you can use a bonus action to toss it aloft and speak its command word. When you do, you also choose how much string to let out, between 10 to 60 feet. The kite then expands to its full size (if it was folded) and creates a cylinder of moderate wind of 10 miles per hour, which allows it to continue flying. The cylinder's height and diameter is the same as the amount of let-out string, up to 60 feet. The kite and top of the cylinder remain centered above your location. You can use another bonus action to speak the flying kite's command word to end the effect and cause it to fall softly back into your hand. You can change the wind's direction at the start of each of your turns for the duration.

You can use the kite to create this wind for up to 1 hour, all at once or in several shorter intervals, each one using a minimum of 1 minute from the duration. The effect ends early if you are no longer holding the string, at which point it flies up to 1d10 times 10 feet in a random direction before landing on the ground. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the string (AC 15) severs the string, causing the kite to fly away as if you let go of the string. The severed string can later be reattached by holding its ends together for 1 minute.
